<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006346 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00139: (2026x Refresh1) Modifying styles of individual symbols

- page_id: `304006346`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006346/2026x+Refresh1+Modifying+styles+of+individual+symbols
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-06-18T11:03:30.719+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize the styles of individual symbols. Using the Symbol Style dialog, you can specify style attributes for specific symbols, determining their appearance.

Changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based symbol styles; the modifications persist even after updating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying style sheets' space='']. To reset, see the procedure.

##### Opening the Symbol Style dialog

To open the Symbol Style dialog

Do one of the following:

- Right-click the selected element symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, click **Symbol Style**. To invoke the Symbol Style dialog for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Docked symbols' space=''], you must right-click the symbol's header.
- Select the element symbol(s) and press Alt+Enter .

##### Modifying individual symbol styles

To modify individual symbol styles

1. Select the symbol(s) whose styles you want to modify and .
2. Locate the attribute you want to modify and change its value to the needed one in the Value column.
3. Click Close .

##### Resetting changes made in the Symbol Style dialog

While changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based symbol styles, you can easily undo them following the procedure below.

To reset changes made in the Symbol Style dialog

1. .
2. Locate the attribute you want to reset to the style of the currently applied style sheet. Its check box in the Default column is deselected. [ATTACHMENT filename='Resetting_changes.png']
3. Select the check box in the Default column for the needed attribute.
4. Click Close .

##### The Symbol Style dialog

The table below lists the style attributes available for modification and their descriptions.

- Modifying a symbol style changes its Default value to false .
- You can hide unwanted symbol styles from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog' space=''] page.

50%

In the **Symbol Style** dialog, symbol styles are ordered into the following groups:

- **Main**: this attribute group displays symbol styles available to the majority of element kinds.
- **Compartment-based attributes**: the availability of specific symbol style groups depends on the element kind, as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space='']. Some of the symbol styles available to individual compartment-based symbol style groups may be the same as in the Main group, while others are compartment-specific. Compartment-based style groups can be:
  - Documentation,
  - Objective,
  - Subject,
  - Actors,
  - Stakeholders,
  - Include Use Cases,
  - Parts,
  - Ports,
  - Parameters,
  - Attributes,
  - Ends,
  - Satisfy Requirements ,
  - Items,
  - Constraints,
  - Require Constraints,
  - Assume Constraints,
  - Assert Constraints,
  - Actions,
  - Perform Actions,
  - Exhibit States,
  - Frames,
  - Enumerations,
  - Metadata.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Name | Value options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Main |  |  |
| Line Width | Positive number | Specify the width of the symbol's outline. |
| Fill Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's header.To enable a gradient for this attribute, enable the view style attribute. |
| Use Fill Color | true/false | Specify whether to use a specific color for the symbol's header. |
| Pen Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's outline. |
| Text Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's text. |
| Font | Multiple font optionsMultiple style options (italic, bold, etc.)Multiple size options | Specify the font style of the symbol's text. |
| Wrap | true/false | Specify whether the text should wrap to fit the width of the element symbol. |
| Compartment Fill Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's compartments. |
| Compartment Text Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color for the text in the symbol's compartments. |
| Show Short Name | true/false | Specify whether the element's should be displayed on the element symbol. |
| Show Name | true/false | Specify whether the element's should be displayed on the element symbol. |
| Show Modifiers | true/false | Specify whether the element's (e.g., in, ref, abstract, etc.) should be displayed on the element symbol. The attribute does not control the individual and portion kind (timeslice, snapshot) modifiers. |
| Show Full Text | true/false | Specify whether element values should be displayed in full or abbreviated. Modifying this attribute affects the values displayed in both the selected symbol's name and its compartments.Modifying this attribute does not affect element values displayed in the metadata compartment. Values in this compartment are always displayed in full.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Content Fill Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's body.To enable a gradient for this attribute, enable the view style attribute.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Text Vertical Position | TopCenterBottom | Specify the element name position for the symbol.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind.The attribute is only effective for element symbols displayed without their compartments. |
| Show Specialization | true/false | Specify whether the textual symbols should be displayed in the element name (e.g., the textual symbol :> or the keyword specializes, in the case of subclassification). The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Qualified Name for Specialization | true/false | Specify whether the specialization's full should be displayed in the element name.Enabling the attribute also requires the 'Show Specialization' attribute to be enabled.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Orientation | HorizontalVertical | Specify the orientation for the symbol.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Path Style | RectilinearObliqueBezier | Specify the style of the path.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Value | true/false | Specify whether the element's defined value should be displayed.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Inherited Sign | true/false | Specify whether the inherited sign should be displayed on the symbol.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Payload | true/false | Specify whether the payload name and sign should be displayed on the connector path.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Text Horizontal Position | LeftCenterRight | Specify the text position for the vertical and horizontal separator symbols. |
| Line Style | SolidDashedDottedetc. | Specify the line style for:the vertical and horizontal separator symbols.the swimlane symbols. |
| Is Rounded | true/false | Specify whether the rectangular shape symbol's corners should be rounded. |
| Show Locale | true/false | Specify whether the annotating element's locale should be displayed on the element symbol. Available for: Comment, Doc. |
| Compartment-based symbol styles (in compartment-based symbol style groups, e.g., Documentation, Objective, Subject, Actors, etc.) |  |  |
| Show Compartment | true/false | Specify whether the compartment should be displayed or suppressed. |
| Show Inherited | true/false | Specify whether the inherited elements should be displayed on the symbol. |
| Show Only Owned | true/false | Specify whether only the owned elements or imported ones, too, should be displayed in the symbol compartment.The attribute is only available for the package symbol and applies to its Members compartment. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can customize the styles of individual symbols. Using the Symbol Style dialog, you can specify style attributes for specific symbols, determining their appearance.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e80d6bd3-c74a-45dd-9c7a-65a10a9ee3ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based symbol styles; the modifications persist even after updating <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[applied style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. To reset, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Resetting changes made in the Symbol Style dialog" /> procedure.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Opening the Symbol Style dialog</h3><p>To open the Symbol Style dialog</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click the selected element symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Symbol Style</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01b07579-7af8-4cc3-aacf-48bc421211e3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To invoke the Symbol Style dialog for a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Docked symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[docked symbol]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, you must right-click the symbol's header.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Select the element symbol(s) and press <strong>Alt+Enter</strong>.</li></ul><h3>Modifying individual symbol styles</h3><p>To modify individual symbol styles</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the symbol(s) whose styles you want to modify and <ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Symbol Style dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[open the Symbol Style dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Locate the attribute you want to modify and change its value to the needed one in the Value column.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Resetting changes made in the Symbol Style dialog</h3><p>While changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based symbol styles, you can easily undo them following the procedure below.</p><p>To reset changes made in the Symbol Style dialog</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Symbol Style dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Symbol Style dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Locate the attribute you want to reset to the style of the currently applied style sheet. Its check box in the <strong>Default </strong>column is deselected.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Resetting_changes.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Select the check box in the <strong>Default </strong>column for the needed attribute.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>The Symbol Style dialog</h3><p>The table below lists the style attributes available for modification and their descriptions. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f005f26-68b4-4ee7-9f09-947dee503a50"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="4878db54-cf23-45f9-8497-eb4527ddc56d">Modifying a symbol style changes its <strong>Default </strong>value to <em>false</em>.</li><li data-uuid="c01759d3-49a4-4dda-a32d-c5ed3585566e">You can hide unwanted symbol styles from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog" /></ac:link> page. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb2e7074-5af7-427c-be26-72d16531ed2a"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5b0f155-eab1-4996-8ff5-c535e498f8d5"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Symbol Style</strong> dialog, symbol styles are ordered into the following groups:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="832f898a-5f9d-4021-a405-f0108ff1180e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Main</strong>: this attribute group displays symbol styles available to the majority of element kinds.</span></li><li data-uuid="9a04570f-15e2-49d7-8017-f3e799236036"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Compartment-based attributes</strong>: the availability of specific symbol style groups depends on the element kind, as <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[each compartment is only available to specific element kinds]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Some of the symbol styles available to individual compartment-based symbol style groups may be the same as in the Main group, while others are compartment-specific. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Compartment-based style groups can be: </span><ul><li data-uuid="296cc312-f05d-4b0f-bfd6-2335f16e98bf"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Documentation, </span></li><li data-uuid="ab48b78d-71fe-46d7-8418-7267146682ae"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Objective, </span></li><li data-uuid="63ba9528-2a75-4bec-974c-6cdeeebf2a4f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Subject, </span></li><li data-uuid="0130c558-4d39-40ff-b311-e8dab08fae47"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Actors, </span></li><li data-uuid="5a9d3834-2216-4cc7-b1cd-2b4c435e0449"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Stakeholders, </span></li><li data-uuid="56c3c666-d1d0-45a9-9df5-6e6d52c61fdf"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Include Use Cases, </span></li><li data-uuid="6947d6ed-5564-4959-9600-d5aa3a2bbba2"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Parts, </span></li><li Sign</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the inherited sign Payload</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the payload <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006343 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00140: (2026x Refresh1) Modifying styles of individual views

- page_id: `304006343`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006343/2026x+Refresh1+Modifying+styles+of+individual+views
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-05-22T10:31:52.066+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The primary ways to customize the styles of individual views:

- Applying style sheets to a specific view.
- Managing style properties for a specific view via the View Style dialog.

Changing the styles for an individual view via the View Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based style sheets; the modifications persist even after updating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying style sheets' space='']. To reset, see the procedure.

##### Applying style sheets to a specific view

- For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying style sheets' space=''] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying style sheets' space=''] page.

##### Managing style properties for a specific view via the View Style dialog

###### Opening the View Style dialog

To open the View Style dialog

- Right-click the selected view. In the shortcut menu, click **View Style**.

###### Modifying an individual view's style

To modify an individual view's style

1. Select the view(s) whose styles you want to modify and .
2. Locate the property you want to modify and change its value to the needed one in the Value column.
3. Click Close .

###### Resetting changes made in the View Style dialog

While changing the styles for an individual view via the View Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based style sheets, you can easily undo them following the procedure below.

To reset changes made in the View Style dialog

1. .
2. Locate the property you want to reset to the style of the currently applied style sheet. Its check box in the Default column is deselected.
3. Select the check box in the Default column for the needed property.
4. Click Close .

###### The View Style dialog

The table below lists the style properties available for modification and their descriptions.

Modifying a view style property changes its **Default**value to *false*.

Currently, other view style properties displayed in the dialog besides the **Use Gradient Fill**are not functional.

| Name | Value options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Main |  |  |
| Use Gradient Fill | true/false | Specify whether a gradient should be used for the Fill Color and Content Fill Color . |
| Show Line Jumps On | NoneHorizontal LineVertical Line | Specify whether line jumps should be used for intersecting lines (e.g., paths).None - line jumps are not used.Horizontal Line - line jumps are used only on horizontal lines (default).Vertical Line - line jumps are used only on vertical lines. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006274 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00141: (2026x Refresh1) Multiplicity

- page_id: `304006274`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006274/2026x+Refresh1+Multiplicity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:18.746+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Element management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Usages can have a multiplicity specifying how many values they can have for the instances of theirfeaturing definition or usage.The multiplicity is indicated as a range of natural numbers in square brackets, e.g., [lowerBound..upperBound].

- The lower bound must be finite. If the lower bound is unspecified, then it is taken to be equal to the upper bound, except when the upper bound is *, in which case it is treated as 0.
- The upper bound must be equal to or greater than the lower bound number. It can be unbounded, indicated by the * sign.

If a multiplicity is not declared for a usage, then the usage inherits the multiplicity constraints of any other usages it subsets or redefines. If no tighter constraint is inherited, the effective default is the most general multiplicity [0..*]. However, a tighter default of [1..1] is implicitly declared for the usage if all of the following conditions hold:

- The usage is an attribute usage, an item usage (including a part usage, except if it is a connection usage), or a port usage.
- The usage is owned by a definition or another usage (not a package).
- The usage does not have any explicit owned subsettings or owned redefinitions.

The following keywords can also be used after the multiplicity or in place of it:

- *nonunique*– a non-unique usage indicates that the same usage value can appear more than once. Usages are unique by default, i.e., two usage values can be the same.
- *ordered*– an ordered usage indicates that the usage values can be placed in an order from 1 to the number of values. The features are unordered by default.

For information on cross multiplicity, which is used only within the context of cross features and is usually specified for the*end*elements of a connector, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Cross features' space=''] page.

##### Specifying usage multiplicity

###### Specifying usage multiplicity via the Textual Editor

To specify usage multiplicity via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, follow the part name with brackets.
2. Specify a range of natural numbers in square brackets, e.g., [lowerBound..upperBound] .
3. Click the Synchronize button.

partbodyAssy:BodyAssy{ 
partdoors[2..4] :Door; 
partbody; 
partsunroof[0..1] :Sunroof; 
}

###### Specifying usage multiplicity via the Specification panel

To specify usage multiplicity via the Specification panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

If the lower bound is unspecified, then it is taken to be equal to the upper bound, except when the upper bound is *, in which case it is treated as 0.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The upper bound must be equal to or greater than the lower bound number. It<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">can be unbounded, indicated by the * sign.</span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If a multiplicity is not declared for a usage, then the usage inherits the multiplicity constraints of any other usages it subsets or redefines. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006124 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00142: (2026x Refresh1) N-ary connection

- page_id: `304006124`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006124/2026x+Refresh1+N-ary+connection
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:05.386+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Structure > (2026x Refresh1) Connection
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An n-ary connection is a complex connector between three or more elements. The n-ary connection is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the connection.

The kind keyword (e.g., *part*) is optional for connection ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Cross features' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating n-ary connections

###### Creating n-ary connections via the Textual Editor

To create n-ary connections via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the connection keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), (optional), then the connect keyword, then state the features explicitly (using the connect...to syntax ) or in a comma-separated list of related features. If the connection has no name and no specialization, the keyword *connection*can be omitted.You can also name connection ends, associating them to features via[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specializations' space=''].
3. Click the Synchronize button.

connectiondefCanBus{ 
endparttransmission:Transmission; 
endpartelectricalPowerController:ElectricalPowerController; 
endpartinternalCombustionEngine:InternalCombustionEngine; 
}

part'power subsystem'{ 
partelectricalPowerController; 
parttransmission; 
partinternalCombustionEngine;

// named specialized (feature typing) connection usage with named endsconnecting features in a comma-separated list: 
connection'can bus':CanBusconnect(t::>transmission,epc::>electricalPowerController,ice::>internalCombustionEngine);

// unnamed unspecialized connection usage connecting featuresin a comma-separated list: 
connect(transmission,electricalPowerController,internalCombustionEngine);}

###### Creating n-ary connections via the view palette

To create n-ary connections via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **n-ary connection** button. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the **connection** or the **connection****def**button and click the **n-ary connection** button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary connection symbol to be created.
3. Select the n-ary connection symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the end command.
4. Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the symbol of the element you want to connect. Repeat for all elements you want to connect.

##### Converting binary and n-ary connectors

###### Converting binary connectors to n-ary via the view

To convert binary connectors to n-ary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the binary connector you want to convert to an n-ary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to N-ary Dot . The binary connector is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.

###### Converting n-aryconnectorstobinaryvia the view

To convert n-ary connectors to binary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the connector you want to convert to a binary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to Path . The n-ary connector dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed. The **Convert to Path**command is only available if the connector has no more than two ends.

##### Creating an n-ary connection definition

###### Creating n-ary connection definitions via the Textual Editor

To create n-ary connection definitions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the connection def keyword.
2. Name the element, then specify the connection definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the connection definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specializations' space=''] of the needed element.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. connectiondefCanBus{ 
endparttransmission:Transmission; 
endpartelectricalPowerController:ElectricalPowerController; 
endpartinternalCombustionEngine:InternalCombustionEngine; 
}

###### Auto-creating n-ary connection definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create n-ary connection definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

###### Creating n-ary connection definitions via the view palette

To create n-ary connection definitions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the**connection def** button. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **connection**or the **n-ary connection******button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the connection def symbol. The connection def symbol is created containing two end element symbols.
  1. Create additional ends via the Create Element button's end command. See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''] page.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006114 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00143: (2026x Refresh1) N-ary interface

- page_id: `304006114`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006114/2026x+Refresh1+N-ary+interface
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:04.793+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Structure > (2026x Refresh1) Interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An n-ary interface is a complex connector between three or more ports. The n-ary interface is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the interface.

- The ends of an interface definition or usage must be port usages.
- The port keyword is optional for interface ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Cross features' space=''] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating n-ary interfaces

###### Creating n-ary interfaces via the Textual Editor

To create n-ary interfaces via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the interface keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specializations' space=''] (optional), then the connect keyword, then state the feature ports explicitly (using the connect...to syntax ) or in a comma-separated list of related features. If the interface has no name and no specialization, the keyword *connect*can be omitted.You can also name interface end ports, associating them to feature ports via[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Reference subsetting' space=''].
3. Click the Synchronize button.

interfacedefCanBusInterface{endporttransmissionPort:CanBusPorts; 
endportelectricalPowerControllerPort:CanBusPorts; 
endportinternalCombustionEnginePort:CanBusPorts; 
}

part'power subsystem'{ 
partelectricalPowerController{portfp;} 
parttransmission{portfp;} 
partinternalCombustionEngine{portfp;}

//named specialized (feature typing) interface usage with named end portsconnecting feature portsin a comma-separated list:interface'can bus':CanBusInterfaceconnect(transmissionPort::>transmission.fp,electricalPowerControllerPort::>electricalPowerController.fp,internalCombustionEnginePort::>internalCombustionEngine.fp);

// unnamed unspecialized interface usage connecting feature portsin a comma-separated list:interface(transmission.fp,electricalPowerController.fp,internalCombustionEngine.fp);}

###### Creating n-ary interfaces via the view palette

To create n-ary interfaces via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the n-ary interface button. If it is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the interface button and click the n-ary interface button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary interface symbol to be created.
3. Select the n-ary interface symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the end command.
4. Draw the end from the n-ary interface symbol to the symbol of the port you want to connect. Repeat for all ports you want to connect.

##### Converting binary and n-ary interfaces

###### Converting binary interface to n-ary via the view

To convert binary interface to n-ary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the binary interface you want to convert to an n-ary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to N-ary Dot . The binary interface is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.

###### Converting n-aryinterfacetobinaryvia the view

To convert n-ary interface to binary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the interface you want to convert to a binary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to Path . The n-ary interface dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed. The **Convert to Path**command is only available if the dependency has no more than two ends.

##### Creating n-ary interface definition

###### Creating n-ary interfaces definition via the Textual Editor

To create n-ary interfaces definition via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the interface def keyword followed by its name.
2. Name the element, then specify the interface definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the interface definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specializations' space=''] of the needed port.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. interfacedefCanBusInterface{endporttransmissionPort:CanBusPorts; 
endportelectricalPowerControllerPort:CanBusPorts; 
endportinternalCombustionEnginePort:CanBusPorts; 
}

###### Auto-creating n-ary interfacedefinition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create n-ary interface definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

###### Creating n-ary interface definitions via the view palette

To create n-ary interface definitions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the**interface def** button. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **interface**or the **n-ary interface**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the interface def symbol. The interface def symbol is created containing two end element symbols.
  1. Create additional ends:
    1. Via the Create Element button's end command. See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''] page.
    2. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click the port button. Click on the interface def symbol to create a port for it.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The n-ary interface is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the interface.</span></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2f2972b-2429-464d-9553-0401e35800cc"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="0cd106ae-0c9d-4643-9d42-f5db7aed3b21">The ends of an interface definition or usage must be port usages.</li><li data-uuid="60b055b1-889f-4a77-afb6-6e9ad045e7b9">The <em>port</em> keyword is optional for interface ends. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006312 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00144: (2026x Refresh1) Occurrences

- page_id: `304006312`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006312/2026x+Refresh1+Occurrences
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-07-01T14:55:16.849+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Common elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An occurrence is used to designate something that has an extent in time, which is the occurrence's lifetime. Occurrences can be structural (parts, items) and behavioral (actions).

##### Creating occurrence usage and definition elements

###### Creating occurrence usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create occurrence usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare one of the following keywords:
  1. *occurrence def*for an occurrence definition.
  2. occurrence for an occurrence usage.
2. Name the element.
3. (Optional) Specify the occurrence features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button. occurrencedefCruiseControlInteraction;// occurrence definition 
occurrencecruiseController;// occurrence usage

###### Creating occurrence usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create occurrence usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. occurrence def to create an occurrence definition element.
  2. occurrence to create an occurrence usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the occurrence features via the Textual Editor.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An occurrence is used to designate something that has an extent in time, which is the occurrence's lifetime. Occurrences can be structural (parts, items) and behavioral (actions). </p><h3>Creating occurrence usage and definition elements</h3><h4>Creating occurrence usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create occurrence usage and definition elements via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1ef64330-8be7-45e8-aa08-da4567f59a2c">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare one of the following keywords:<ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span> </span><em>occurrence def</em><span> </span>for an occurrence definition.</p></li><li><span> </span><em>occurrence </em>for an occurrence usage.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="f2b55c9e-b211-48b5-96fc-912dd65c904a">Name the element.</li><li data-uuid="ecf75f98-a7e8-4efd-8494-5ea8a22aadc8">(Optional) Specify the occurrence features in the element body.</li><li data-uuid="982536c9-6840-4a48-86ce-1bf99d45d838">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03f18383-07ac-491a-8792-cb0c1f4eda9a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">occurrence</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CruiseControlInteraction</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// occurrence definition</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">occurrence</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cruiseController</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;              </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// occurrence usage</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating occurrence usage and definition elements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create occurrence usage and definition elements via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="6647ec7e-7bec-492a-8888-548ad7798000"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li data-uuid="7673280f-b7c0-4f8a-a617-d94d6dac6ea6">In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select one of the following:<ol><li><strong>occurrence def</strong> to create an occurrence definition element.</li><li><strong>occurrence </strong>to create an occurrence usage element.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="1567c61a-6dd4-4661-82f9-543db359bef9">Name the element.</li><li data-uuid="4c81f81c-3be7-4ca3-bbf2-837f187f4bb1">(Optional) Specify the occurrence features via the Textual Editor.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005159 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00145: (2026x Refresh1) Open in Table

- page_id: `304005159`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005159/2026x+Refresh1+Open+in+Table
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-05-06T22:11:51.303+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Creating views > (2026x Refresh1) Tabular views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Open in Table** command allows you to view the selected element's content in any tabular view available in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Activating View Creation dialog' space=''] **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom View Creation dialog' space='SYSML2PTWRT']**dialog without creating a tabular view in the model. It recursively exposes the selected element's content and automatically [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Filtering elements for views' space='']. The table itself is not a model element, and it is marked as Temporary. Closing the temporary table and opening it anew via the **Open in Table** command opens a new temporary table.

You can perform all the same actions as in regular tables, except for:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns' space='']Manage columns via the **Columns** button
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing tabular view rows' space='']Remove elements from the table via the **Remove From Table** command

Any table-specific modifications ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in tabular views' space=''], column [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns' space=''], etc.) do not persist.

##### Opening element content in temporary tables

To open element content in temporary tables

1. Right-click an element in the Containment tree or an element symbol in a view.
2. In the element shortcut menu, click the Open in Table command.
3. In the dropdown menu, select the needed table type. The selected element's content is recursively displayed in the selected temporary table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">The <strong>Open in Table</strong> command allows you to view the selected element's content in any tabular view available in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Activating View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[active]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2PTWRT" ri:content-title="Custom View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[View Creation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog without creating a tabular view in the model. It recursively exposes the selected element's content and automatically <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying filter conditions"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filters out KerML library elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The table itself is not a model element, and it is marked as Temporary. Closing the temporary table and opening it anew via the <strong>Open in Table</strong> command opens a new temporary table.</p><p style="text-align: left;">You can perform all the same actions as in regular tables, except for: </p><ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="40973884-2dfa-436f-a420-6190c6967cb8"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns" the <strong>Remove From Table</strong> command</ac:link-body></ac:link></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35d9bf33-8598-4191-a8bf-c46a0dcfd7f8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Any table-specific modifications (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in tabular views" persist. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Opening element content in temporary tables</h3><p>To open element content in temporary tables</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="f1368424-afc6-4d48-ba2f-475a37328d77">Right-click an element in the Containment tree or an element symbol in a view.</li><li data-uuid="3e35cbaa-ddb0-4cad-b1f8-15fadfdd41fe">In the element shortcut menu, click the <strong>Open in Table</strong> command.</li><li data-uuid="93ad8413-744d-4f2d-a801-f98cf92ee03b">In the dropdown menu, select the needed table type.<br />The selected <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=312246743 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00146: (2026x Refresh1) Opening used projects

- page_id: `312246743`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/312246743/2026x+Refresh1+Opening+used+projects
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-06-10T10:45:17.548+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Project management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Managing local used projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Projects can only use other projects in read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as a stand-alone. You can do this from within the main project in which the project is used by following the procedures listed below.

###### Opening a used project via the Containment tree

To open a used project via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, expand the Used Projects node.
2. Right-click a used project and click Open Latest Version . The used project is opened. [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening a used project via the Containment tree.png']

###### Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog

To open a used project via the Project Usages dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing local used projects' space='']Open the **Project Usages**dialog .
2. In the Project Usages dialog, select a used project, then on the right side section of the dialog, click the Open Latest Version button.
3. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. The used project is opened. [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Projects can only use other projects in read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as a stand-alone. You can do this from within the main project in which the project is used by following the procedures listed below.</p><h4>Opening a used project via the Containment tree</h4><p>To open a used project via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="156ce0df-3f70-495d-a881-460fe041c520">In the Containment tree, expand the<span> </span><strong>Used Projects</strong><span> </span>node.</li><li data-uuid="7d7570fb-70f2-4fce-9b6c-ca3ef2bcd1b0">Right-click a used project and click <strong>Open Latest Version</strong>.<br />The used project is opened.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening a used project via the Containment tree.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h4>Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog</h4><p>To open a used project via the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="2fe95945-9d51-417b-b8e3-5a09e832e257"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing local used projects" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="a4567379-e0c9-413b-9dd1-b9c067a932a0">In the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog, select a used project, then on the right side section of the dialog, click the <strong>Open Latest Version</strong> button.</li><li data-uuid="77d75c32-a4ef-419d-9d11-c6a3717e6692">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.<br />The used project is opened.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog.png" /></ac:image></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005114 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00147: (2026x Refresh1) Opening views

- page_id: `304005114`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005114/2026x+Refresh1+Opening+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:08.408+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the procedures on this page for opening views.

##### Opening views

###### Opening views via the Containment tree

Opening vews via the Containment tree

1. Select the view element in the Containment tree and do one of the following:
  1. Double-click the view element.
  2. Right-click the view element and in the shortcut menu, click Open or Open in New Tab .
  3. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+Enter or Enter . **Open**is equivalent to **Ctrl+Enter**. The commands open the selected view in place of the currently active view.**Open in New Tab**is equivalent to **Enter**. The commands open the selected view in a new view tab.

###### Opening views via the Textual Editor

Opening views via the Textual Editor

- In the Textual Editor, right-click the view you want to open and in the shortcut menu, click Open View .

###### Opening views via the view element symbol in a view

Opening views via the view element symbol in a view

1. Select the view element symbol in a view and do one of the following:
  1. Double-click the view element symbol.
  2. Right-click the view element symbol and in the shortcut menu, click Open or Open in New Tab .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>See the procedures on this page for opening views.</p><h3>Opening views</h3><h4>Opening views via the Containment tree</h4><p>Opening vews via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the view element in the Containment tree and do one of the following:<ol><li>Double-click the view element.</li><li>Right-click the view element and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Open</strong> or <strong>Open in New Tab</strong>.</li><li>On the keyboard, press <strong>Ctrl+Enter </strong>or <strong>Enter</strong>. <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6392ba39-8ce6-4a63-97fc-6dc431efd7dd"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><strong>Open </strong>is equivalent to <strong>Ctrl+Enter</strong>. The commands open the selected view in place of the currently active view. </li><li><strong>Open in New Tab </strong>is equivalent to <strong>Enter</strong>. The commands open the selected view in a new view tab.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ol><h4>Opening views via the Textual Editor</h4><p>Opening views via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="2ec863d2-d2d6-4f29-9fdd-ef68c247b40d">In the Textual Editor, right-click the view you want to open and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Open View</strong>.</li></ul><h4>Opening views via the view element symbol in a view</h4><p>Opening views via the view element symbol in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the view element symbol in a view and do one of the following:<ol><li>Double-click the view element symbol.</li><li>Right-click the view element symbol and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Open</strong> or <strong>Open in New Tab</strong>.</li></ol></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006295 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00148: (2026x Refresh1) Package

- page_id: `304006295`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006295/2026x+Refresh1+Package
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:21.588+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Common elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Packages are namespaces that contain member elements that may be owned or unowned. It is used as a container for organizing other elements within the model. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Key concepts' space=''] page.Additionally, a package can import members of another package as unowned members. You can also filter imported elements based on certain conditions.

##### Creating package elements

###### Creating a package element via the Textual Editor

To create a package element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *package*.
  2. *library package* .
2. Specify the element name.
3. (Optional) .
4. Click the Synchronize button. library package Policies; // library package elementpackage'Vehicle Features'{ // package element 
privateimportPolicies::**;filter@SysML::Systems::PartUsage;}

###### Creating a package element via the Create Element command

To create a package element via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click a root namespace, and in the shortcut menu, click Create Element .
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. package to create a package element.
  2. library package to create a library package element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) .

##### Specifying packages

###### Importing elements to packages

An import relationship allows one package to import memberships from another package. The member elements from imported memberships become (unowned) members of the importing package in addition to being members of the imported package. In particular, this allows members of the imported package to be referenced in textual notations within the scope of the importing package without having to qualify the member names with the name of the imported package.

- For more information and procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing and filtering elements' space=''] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing and filtering elements' space=''] page.

###### Specifying element import filters

Packages have the capability to filter elements upon import based on certain conditions, usually defined in terms of the metadata provided by annotations of those elements. Only elements that meet all filter conditions become imported members of the packages.

- For more information and procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing and filtering elements' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing and filtering elements' space=''] page.

###### Package members display in symbolic views

- To display package members in the Members compartment of the package symbol, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''].
- To control whether the Members compartment displays only owned members or imported ones as well, use the **Show Only Owned** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Packages are namespaces that contain member elements that may be owned or unowned. It is used as a container for organizing other elements within the model. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For more information, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Package"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Key concepts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Key concepts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page. </span>Additionally, a package can import members of another package as unowned members.  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// library package element</span>  </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Features'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>{  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// package element</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   <span> </span></span>private<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span>import<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Policies</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**; The member elements from imported memberships become (unowned) members of the importing package in addition to being members of the imported package. In particular, this allows members of the imported package to be referenced in textual notations within the scope of the importing package without having to qualify the member names with the name of the imported package. </span></p><ul><li><span>For more information and procedures, see the </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Importing elements"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing and filtering elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Importing elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedure on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing and filtering elements" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul><h4>Specifying element import filters </h4><p>Packages have the capability to filter elements upon import based on certain conditions, usually defined in terms of the metadata provided by annotations of those elements. Only elements that meet all filter conditions become imported members of the packages. </p><ul><li><span>For more information and procedures, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Element import filtering"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing and filtering elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Element import filtering]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing and filtering elements" /></ac:link> page.</span></li></ul><h4><span>Package members display in symbolic views</span></h4><ul><li data-uuid="7d57743d-bdc0-4eed-8626-602323185313"><span>To display package members in the Members compartment of the package symbol, <ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying and suppressing compartments"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[display the compartment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="0f7b1a13-9ae9-458c-9368-ceec5c91e2c7"><span>To control whether the Members compartment displays only owned members or imported ones as well, use the <strong>Show Only Owned</strong> <ac:link ac:anchor="The Symbol Style dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[symbol style]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005981 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00149: (2026x Refresh1) Parallel states

- page_id: `304005981`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005981/2026x+Refresh1+Parallel+states
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:54.538+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Behavior > (2026x Refresh1) States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A state definition or usage may hierarchically contain state usages in its body. The performance of these substates depends on whether the containing state is parallel or non-parallel:

- In non-parallel states (the default), substate performances occur sequentially, meaning only one substate is active at any given time. Transitions control the activation of these substates.
- In parallel states (triggered by adding the keyword parallel before the body), substates run concurrently, with their performances overlapping in time. No transitions are allowed between concurrent states.

Contained substates may also decompose further, employing transitions in sequential states.

##### Specifying parallel states

###### Specifying parallel states via the Textual Editor

To specify parallel states via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, declare the keyword parallel before the state's body.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

stateonparallel{ // The following substates are performed concurrentlystatespeed{ ...} // Any contained substates are performed sequentially 
stateheat{ ...} // Any contained substates are performed sequentially 
}

###### Specifying parallel states via the element shortcut menu

To specify parallel states via the element shortcut menu

- Right-click a state element in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in its shortcut menu, in the Properties group, select the Is Parallel property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The performance of these substates depends on whether the containing state is parallel or non-parallel:</p><ul><li>In <strong>non-parallel states</strong> (the default), substate performances occur sequentially, meaning only one substate is active at any given time. Transitions control the activation of these substates.</li><li>In <strong>parallel states</strong> (triggered by adding the keyword <em>parallel</em> before the body), substates run concurrently, with their performances overlapping in time. No transitions are allowed between concurrent states.</li></ul><p>Contained substates may also decompose further, employing transitions in sequential states.</p><h3>Specifying parallel states</h3><h4>Specifying parallel states via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify parallel states via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, declare the keyword <em>parallel</em> <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005946 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00150: (2026x Refresh1) Part decomposition

- page_id: `304005946`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005946/2026x+Refresh1+Part+decomposition
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:52.809+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Structure > (2026x Refresh1) Parts
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Part definitions and part usages can be decomposed allowing you to build elaborate model structures composed of related parts. You can create a parts tree representing a composition of a part from other parts.

##### Decomposing part definitions

You can represent the next level of decomposition of a part definition using the feature typing (*defined by*) relationship.

###### Decomposing part definitions via the Textual Editor

To decompose part definitions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the part usage that you want to define, nested within a part definition.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Feature typing' space=''] .

partdefVehicle{ 
partengine:Engine; 
parttransmission:Transmission; 
partdriveshaft:Driveshaft; 
partrearAxleAssembly:RearAxleAssembly;// part usage, owned by a part definition, defined by another part definitionpartfuelTank:FuelTank; 
} 
partdefRearAxleAssembly{ 
partdifferential:Differential; 
partrearAxle:Axle; 
partrearWheel1:Wheel; 
partrearWheel2:Wheel; 
}

###### Displaying decompositions of part definitions in a view

To display decompositions of part definitions in a view

- In a view, select the part definition symbol and the part usage symbol it defines, then use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command. The feature typing relationship is displayed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Decomposing part usages

You can display a parts tree representing a composition of a part from other parts.

###### Decomposing part usages via the Textual Editor

To decompose part usages via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the part you want to decompose, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating parts' space=''] .
2. Click the Synchronize button.

partvehicle_b : Vehicle{ // the owning part usage with nested part usages 
partengine:Engine{ 
partcyl:Cylinder; 
} 
parttransmission:Transmission; 
partfuelTank:FuelTank; 
partrearAxleAssembly:AxleAssembly; 
}

###### Decomposing part usages via a view by creating a composite part

To decompose part usages via a view by creating a composite part

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating parts' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the part for which you want to create a composite part.
2. Create a composite part in one of the following ways:
  1. Via the view palette (part symbol option):
    1. In the view palette, under the parts group, click the part button to create a part usage element.
    2. Click on the part symbol displayed in the view pane to create a part owned by another.
  2. Via the view palette (feature membership option):
    1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the feature membership button.
    2. First, click on the part symbol displayed in the view pane and then click elsewhere in the view pane to create a part owned by another.
  3. Via the smart manipulator (feature membership option):
    1. Click the part symbol for which you want to create a composite part.
    2. In its smart manipulator, click the feature membership icon , then click elsewhere in the view pane to create a part owned by another.

###### Decomposing part usages via a view by designating an existing part as a composite one

To decompose part usages via a view by designating an existing part as a composite one

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating parts' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the parts you want to designate as the owner and the owned.
2. Designate an existing part as owned by another in one of the following ways:
  1. Via the view palette (feature membership option):
    1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the feature membership button.
    2. First, click on the part symbol you want to designate as the owner, then the one you want to designate as the owned one.
  2. Via the smart manipulator (feature membership option):
    1. Click the part symbol you want to designate as the owner.
    2. In its smart manipulator, click the feature membership icon, then click on the part you want to designate as the owned one.

###### Displaying decompositions of part usages in a view

To display decompositions of part usages in a view

- In a view, select the owning part usage symbol and use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command under the Tree-View Style category. The feature membership and the owned features (part usages) are displayed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Reusing and modifying the parts tree

A part can subset another part, facilitating parts tree reuse and modification. The subsetting part inherits the part decomposition and other features. You can then modify the inherited parts and features through redefinition and subsetting in addition to adding new parts.

###### Reusing and modifying the parts tree via the Textual Editor

To reuse and modify the parts tree via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating parts' space=''] you want to reuse, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Subsetting' space=''] it.
2. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Redefinition' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Subsetting' space=''] the part features in the element body.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

partvehicle_b: Vehicle{// subsetted part 
partengine:Engine{ 
partcyl[4..8] :Cylinder; 
} 
parttransmission:Transmission; 
partfuelTank:FuelTank; 
partrearAxleAssembly:AxleAssembly; 
} 
partvehicle_c:> vehicle_b{ // a part reuses the parts tree by subsetting another part 
parteng:>engine{ 
partcy:>cyl[4]; 
} 
}

###### Reusing the parts tree via the view palette

To reuse the parts tree via the view palette

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating parts' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the part whose decomposition you want to reuse.
2. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, click the subsetting button.
3. Click on the part you want to denote as the subsetting one and then click on the part you want to denote as the subsetted (reused) one.

###### Displaying the parts tree reuse in a view

To display the parts tree reuse in a view

1. In a view, select the subsetting and the subsetted part usage symbols, then u se the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command . The subsetting relationship is displayed.
2. (Optional) To display owned features of the subsetting or subsetted parts, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command. The owned features (part usages) are displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='Parts tree_reuse_and_modification.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelTank</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rearAxleAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">AxleAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Decomposing part usages via a view by creating a composite part</h4><p>To decompose part usages via a view by creating a composite part</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating parts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[display]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the part for which you want to create a composite part.</li><li>Create a composite part in one of the following ways:<ol><li>Via the view palette (part symbol option):<ol><li>In the view palette, under the parts group, click the <strong>part</strong><span> button to create a part usage element.</span></li><li>Click on the part symbol displayed in the view pane to create a part owned by another. </li></ol></li><li>Via the view palette (feature membership option):<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the <strong>feature membership</strong> <span>button.</span></li><li>First, click on the part symbol displayed in the view pane and then click <span>elsewhere</span> in the view pane to create a part owned by another.</li></ol></li><li>Via the smart manipulator (feature membership option):<br /><ol><li>Click the part symbol for which you want to create a composite part.</li><li>In its smart manipulator, click the <strong>feature membership</strong> icon<span>, then click elsewhere in the view pane </span>to create a part owned by another.</li></ol></li></ol></li></ol><h4>Decomposing part usages via a view by designating an existing part as a composite one</h4><p>To decompose part usages via a view by designating an existing part as a composite one</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating parts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[display]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the parts you want to designate as the owner and the owned.</li><li>Designate an existing part as owned by another in one of the following ways:<ol><li>Via the view palette (feature membership option):<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the <strong>feature membership</strong> <span>button.</span></li><li>First, click on the part symbol you want to designate as the owner, then the one you want to designate as the owned one.</li></ol></li><li>Via the smart manipulator (feature membership option):<br /><ol><li>Click the part symbol you want to designate as the owner.</li><li>In its smart manipulator, click the <strong>feature membership</strong> icon, then click on the part you want to designate as the owned one.</li></ol></li></ol></li></ol><h4>Displaying decompositions of part usages in a view</h4><p>To display decompositions of part usages in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>In a view, select the owning part usage symbol and use the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Features]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>command under the <strong>Tree-View Style</strong> category. <br />The feature membership and the owned features (part usages) are displayed. </li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Decomposing_a_part_usage .png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Part decomposition" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Reusing and modifying the parts tree</h3><p>A part can subset another part, facilitating parts tree reuse and modification. The subsetting part inherits the part decomposition and other features. You can then modify the inherited parts and features through redefinition and subsetting in addition to adding new parts.</p><h4>Reusing and modifying the parts tree via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To reuse and modify the parts tree via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating parts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[declare the part usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> you want to reuse, then <ac:link ac:anchor="Subsetting a usage via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subset]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> it.</li><li>(Optional) <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subset]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the part features in the element body.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8811e49e-ed80-4671-a9c5-db96a9276d25"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle </span>{<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                                     <span Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display In-Between Relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">command</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. The subsetting relationship is displayed.</span></li><li>(Optional) To display owned features of the subsetting or subsetted parts, use the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Features]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>command. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005939 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00151: (2026x Refresh1) Part interconnection

- page_id: `304005939`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005939/2026x+Refresh1+Part+interconnection
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:52.269+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Structure > (2026x Refresh1) Parts
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Parts can be connected to define their interactions and relationships within a model. The primary methods for connecting parts are:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Connection' space=''] (i.e., without ports) that specify a higher level of abstraction, likely establishing physical or logical connections.
  - Parts may also be connected using:
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Flow' space=''] connections
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Binding' space=''] connections

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Parts connected via a connection.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Interface' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Port' space=''] (i.e., with ports) that specify more detailed, formalized interactions.
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Port' space=''] on parts must be compatible with the interface definition typing the interface usage that connects the parts via ports.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Parts connected through ports via an interface.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Parts can be connected to define their interactions and relationships within a model. The primary methods for connecting parts are: </p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Connection" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connections]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (i.e., without ports) that specify a higher level of abstraction, likely establishing physical or logical connections. <ul><li>Parts may also be connected using: <ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Flow" /></ac:link> connections</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Binding" /></ac:link> connections</li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_connection.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Part interconnection" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Parts connected via a connection.</h6><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interfaces]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (i.e., with ports) that specify more detailed, formalized interactions.<ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Port" /></ac:link> on parts must be compatible with the interface definition typing the interface usage that connects the parts via ports.</li></ul></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_interface.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Part interconnection" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Parts connected through ports via an interface.</h6>
````

## PAGE 00152: (2026x Refresh1) Parts
