<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006266 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00153: (2026x Refresh1) Perform action

- page_id: `304006266`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006266/2026x+Refresh1+Perform+action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:18.032+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Behavior > (2026x Refresh1) Actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A perform action usage specifies that an action is performed by the owner of the performed action usage. A perform action usage is referential, which allows the performed action behavior to be defined in a different context than that of the performer.

A perform action usage can be a feature of:

- a part definition or usage, specifying that the referenced action is performed by the containing part.
- an action definition or usage, whereby the perform action usage represents a referential "call" from the containing action to the performed action.

There are two ways an action can be performed:

50%

- by referencing the action usage defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the perform keyword (unnamed element) or the perform action keyword (named element). [ATTACHMENT filename='perform_symbol.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='perform_action_symbol_2.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 

50%

- by containing an owned perform action usage, i.e., the performed action is the perform action usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the perform action keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='perform_action_symbol.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

##### Performing an action by referencing an action usage defined elsewhere in the model

###### Performing actions via the Textual Editor1Performing actions via the Textual Editor

To perform actions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to perform an action, do one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed perform action, declare the perform keyword followed by the name of the performed action.
  2. For a named one, declare the perform action keyword, specify the element name, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Reference subsetting' space=''] the performed action.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

action 'run mission'; // referenced action

part'drone in any mission'{

// unnamed perform action referencing an action defined elsewhere in the modelperform'run mission';

// named perform action referencing an action defined elsewhere in the modelperformaction'run delivery mission' references'run mission';performaction'run delivery mission'::>'run mission';

}

###### Performing actions via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To perform actions via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag an action element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on:
  - the target element symbol that you want to perform the action by referencing it using the **Create Perform Action** command.
  - the perform action usage symbol that you want to perform the action by referencing it using the **Set Performed Action** command.

###### Performing actions via the view palette

To perform actions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click perform . If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the**perform****action**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. To perform an existing action, in the view pane, click on the part element symbol, then on the action element symbol.
  2. To create a new perform action, in the view pane, click on the part element symbol, then elsewhere in the view pane to create an action element symbol. The perform path is drawn between the element symbols.

###### Performing actions via the smart manipulator in a view

To perform actions via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view , do one of the following:
  - To perform an existing action, select the part element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the perform command. Click on the action element symbol.
  - To perform a new action, select the part element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the perform command. Click elsewhere in the view pane to create an action element symbol . The perform path is drawn between the element symbols.

###### Performing actionsvia the Create Relation command

To perform actions via the Create Relation command

1. Right-click on the client (source) element.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click perform . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the supplier (target). Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the supplier (target) and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the supplier (target) from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

###### Performing actionsvia the Specification panel

To perform actions via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop an action element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on the **Performed Action** field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the perform action element. The appropriate action is specified for the element.

##### Performing an action via the owned perform action usageitselfas the performed action

###### Performing actions via the Textual Editor

To perform actions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to perform an action, declare the perform action keyword followed by its name.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

part'drone in any mission'{

// performed action is the defined perform action usage itself contained by the parentperformaction'run mission';

}

###### Performing actions via the Create Element command

To perform actions via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select the perform action element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the perform action's features.

###### Performing actions via the view palette2Performing actions via the view palette

To perform actions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click the perform action button.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the perform action symbol.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the perform action's features.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A perform action usage specifies that an action is performed by the owner of the performed action usage. A perform action usage is referential, which allows the performed action behavior to be defined in a different context than that of the performer.</span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A perform action usage can be a feature of:</span></p><ul><li>a part definition or usage, specifying that the referenced action is performed by the containing part. </li><li>an action definition or usage, whereby the perform action usage represents a referential &quot;call&quot; from the containing action to the performed action.</li></ul><p>There are two ways an action can be performed:</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fdf8846c-d06e-427f-8430-f2a5f5fbba35"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8899d278-2a90-44e6-8cf6-1e691b465077"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="d2f275fc-8c91-4e51-a708-d1ae1ee8a3a0">by <strong>referencing </strong>the action usage defined elsewhere in the model. <ul><li>The element symbol is displayed with the <em>perform</em> keyword (unnamed element) or the <em>perform action</em><span> keyword (named element).<br /></span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="perform_symbol.png" /></ac:image><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="perform_action_symbol_2.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="d29a7541-2117-4799-9dd9-3eca5215a9f4">See the procedures:<ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Performing an action by referencing an action usage defined elsewhere in the model" />:<ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Performing actions via the Textual Editor1"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Performing actions via the Textual Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Performing actions via the Drag and Drop handler in a view" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Performing actions via the view palette" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Performing actions via the smart manipulator in a view" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Performing actions via the Create Relation command" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Performing actions via the Specification panel" /></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="647d2738-f294-4137-98af-f986d7e96689"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="54fd59b0-9d99-4023-a571-bc6e8a793c5c">by <strong>containing </strong>an owned perform action usage, i.e., the performed action is the perform action usage itself.<ul><li>The element symbol is displayed with the <em>perform action</em> keyword.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="perform_action_symbol.png" /></ac:image></li><li>See the procedures: <ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Performing an action via the owned perform action usage itself as the performed action "><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Performing an action via the owned perform action usage itself as the performed action]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:<ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Performing actions via the Textual Editor" /> </li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Performing actions via the Create Element command" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Performing actions via the view palette2"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Performing actions via the view palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Performing an action by referencing an action usage defined elsewhere in the model</h3><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94603f46-95ee-4295-90e3-9745536df2d6"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Performing actions via the Textual Editor1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Performing actions via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To perform actions via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to perform an action, do one of the following:<ol><li class="auto-cursor-target"><span>For an unnamed perform action, declare the </span><em>perform </em><span>keyword followed by the name of the performed action. </span></li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><span>For a named one, </span><span>declare the </span><em>perform action </em><span>keyword, specify the element name, then <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Reference subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reference subset]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the performed action.</span></li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0179859c-8caf-49fb-8c29-bbc387040dec"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span>;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// referenced action</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed perform action referencing an action defined elsewhere in the model<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named perform action referencing an action defined elsewhere in the model<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run delivery mission' <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">references</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run delivery mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Performing actions via the Drag and Drop handler in a view </h4><p>To perform actions via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li><span>Drag an action element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler" /></ac:link> and drop it on:</span><ul><li><span>the target element symbol that you want to perform the action </span><span>by referencing it </span><span>using the</span><span> <strong>Create Perform Action </strong></span><span>command.</span><span> </span></li><li><span>the perform action usage symbol that you want to perform the action </span><span>by referencing it </span><span>using the</span><span> <strong>Set Performed Action </strong></span><span>command.</span><span> </span></li></ul></li></ul><h4>Performing actions via the view palette</h4><p><span>To perform actions via the view palette</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="56d8f301-940e-436a-9248-6fcfd1eab62b">In the view palette, under the Actions group, click <strong>perform</strong>.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b02da26b-933f-494b-9d1f-46da8cbdc3b4"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the<span> <strong>perform </strong></span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">action<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="13f4a5db-a0e7-46df-8edc-440aa139ecae">Do one of the following:<ol><li><span>To perform an existing action, in the view pane, click on the part element symbol, then on the action element symbol. </span></li><li><span>To create a new perform action, in the view pane, click on the part element symbol, then elsewhere in the view pane to create an action element symbol. <br /></span>The <strong>perform</strong><span> path is drawn between the element symbols.</span></li></ol></li></ol><h4><span>Performing actions via the smart manipulator in a view</span></h4><p><span>To perform actions via the smart manipulator in a view</span></p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="32ba9d2d-4eca-464e-90d0-7175a47fad59">In a<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">view</span>, do one of the following:<ul><li><span>To perform an existing action, </span>select the part element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the<strong><span> perform </span></strong>command. Click on the action element symbol.</li><li><span>To perform a new action, </span>select the part element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the<strong><span> perform </span></strong>command. Click <span>elsewhere in the view pane to create an action element symbol</span>.<br />The <strong>perform</strong><span> path is drawn between the element symbols.</span></li></ul></li></ul><h4><span>Performing actions </span><span>via the Create Relation command</span></h4><p>To perform actions via the Create Relation command</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="cde67f00-4df1-4301-8293-2445c0ed4a08">Right-click on the client (source) element.</li><li data-uuid="b881b775-dc4e-4170-8563-97289870ce95">In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Relation</strong>, then in the submenu, under <strong>Outgoing</strong>, click <strong>perform</strong>. The <strong>Create... </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="fc10845c-3568-4ad8-8abb-4d5f8dd2f5de">In the <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the supplier (target). Hold Shift to select multiple elements.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the supplier (target) and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the supplier (target) from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Create... </strong>dialog.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="c8d316de-87de-4190-9ec2-6c3cad8cc6f0">In the <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h4>Performing actions<span> via the Specification panel</span></h4><p>To perform actions via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag and drop an action element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler" /></ac:link> and drop it on the<span> <strong>Performed Action</strong></span><strong><span> </span></strong>field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the perform action element. <span><br /></span>The appropriate action is specified for the element.</li></ul><h3><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Performing an action via the owned perform action usage </span><span>itself </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">as the performed action </span></h3><h4>Performing actions via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To perform actions via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to perform an action, declare the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">perform action </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">keyword followed by its name.</span></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8e3dcb4-b540-4205-af85-28339128c8cb"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// performed action is the defined perform action usage itself contained by the parent<br /></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Performing actions via the Create Element command</h4><p>To perform actions via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select the <strong>perform action </strong>element.</li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Specify the perform action's features.</li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="269bd4ec-ac7e-44b3-8055-32f076bc4b0f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Performing actions via the view palette2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Performing actions via the view palette</h4><p>To perform actions via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Actions group, click the <strong>perform action </strong><span>button.</span></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the perform action symbol.</li><li>Name the element.</li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Specify the perform action's features. </span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314181533 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00154: (2026x Refresh1) Performing SysML v1 to v2 model transformation

- page_id: `314181533`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314181533/2026x+Refresh1+Performing+SysML+v1+to+v2+model+transformation
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-06-25T11:43:58.758+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 to SysML v2 transition > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 to v2 model conversion > (2026x Refresh1) Transforming the SysML v1 model to v2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration plugin to enable model transformation features, allowing you to transform SysML v1 models to SysML v2.The migration of SysML v1 models to SysML v2 is done by exporting a SysML v1 project to a local SysML v2 project with the file name '<project_name>_migrated'.

#### NOTE: Prerequisite

Prerequisite

The SysML v1 to v2 Migration plugin, which is available in the Resource Manager via the *<tool_installation_directory>\data\resourcemanager\distribution\core.rdzip* file, must be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Installation and system requirements' space=''] in the modeling tool.

##### Migrating SysML v1 to v2

To export a SysML v1 project to a local SysML v2 project file

- In the main menu, click File > Export To > SysML v2 Model . Specify the project name and location in the Save window and click Save . The exported project is opened in the modeling tool. Migration statusIf failures occur during the migration, a migration status file *MigrationReportOf<projectName>*.*xlsx* is generated. The link to the file is displayed in the Notification window. The file contains two sheets: 'Not Migrated Elements' and 'Not Migrated Element Properties', listing failed element and property migrations, respectively.

##### SysML v1 to v2 element migration map

#### WARNING: SysML v1 to v2 element migration - a work in progress

SysML v1 to v2 element migration - a work in progress

- Currently, only partial SysML v1 element migration is supported by the modeling tool, as SysML v1 to v2 element migration is a work in progress, covering over 80% of the SysML v1 to SysML v2 Transformation specification.
- Diagram migration currently supports only symbolic diagram to view usage migration. Tables, maps, matrices, etc., will be supported in future releases.

See the table below for elements covered by the migration.

| SysML v1 input | Result in the SysML v2 model |
| --- | --- |
| Block | Part Definition |
| Package | Package |
| Comment | Comment |
| Generalization | Subclassification |
| Interface Block | Interface Definition |
| Proxy Port | Port Usage |
| Port | Port Usage |
| ~Interface Block | Conjugated Port Definition |
| Signal | Item Definition |
| Flow Property | Item Usage |
| Part Property | Part Usage |
| Value Property | Attribute Usage |
| Constraint Property | Assert Constraint Usage / Calculation UsageAssert Constraint Usage if the type in the target model is a Constraint Definition.Calculation Usage if the type in the target model is a Calculation Definition. |
| Reference Property | Attribute Usage |
| Value Type | Attribute Definition |
| Connector | Connection Usage / Interface Usage |
| Connector End | Reference Usage (IsEnd=true) |
| Requirement | Requirement Usage |
| Enumeration | Enumeration Definition |
| Enumeration Literal | Enumeration Usage |
| Dependency | Dependency |
| Use case | Use Case Definition |
| Include | Include Use Case Usage |
| Extend | No alternative match |
| ExtensionPoint | No alternative match |
| Actor | Part Usage owned via Actor Membership |
| Activity | Action Definition |
| Call Behavior Action | Action Usage |
| Structured Activity Node | Action Usage |
| Activity Node | abstract |
| Input Pin | Reference Usage (Direction=in) |
| Output Pin | Reference Usage (Direction=out) |
| Action Input Pin | Reference Usage (Direction=in) |
| Pin | abstract |
| Value Pin | Reference Usage (Direction=in) |
| Fork Node | Fork Node |
| Join Node | Join Node |
| Merge Node | Merge Node |
| Decision Node | Decision Node |
| Parameter | Reference Usage |
| Behavior | abstract |
| Object Flow | Succession Flow Usage / Flow UsageIf the object flow end is an activity parameter node, it is migrated to a binding connector. |
| Object Node | abstract |
| Control Flow | Succession Usage / Transition Usage |
| Activity Edge | abstract |
| Transition | Transition Usage |
| State | State Usage |
| State Machine | State Definition |
| Region | State Usage |
| Trigger | Payload of Accept Action Usage |
| Central Buffer Node | Action Usage |
| Activity Parameter Node | No alternative match |
| Opaque Expression | Calculation Usage |
| Send Signal Action | Send Action Usage |
| Accept Event Action | Accept Action Usage |
| Item/Information Flow | Flow Usage / Flow Definition |
| Binding Connector | Binding Connector As Usage |
| Association | Connection Definition / Interface Definition |
| Class | Occurrence Definition |
| Satisfy | Satisfy Requirement Usage |
| Derive | Connector + Metadata |
| Verify | Requirement Verification Membership |
| Property | Reference UsageIf the property:Has no type: Reference usageTyped by data type: Attribute usageTyped by signal: Item usage Typed by class: Occurrence usageTyped by activity: Action usage Typed by a state machine: State usage Typed by use case: Use case usage Typed by interface: Port usageIf the property is typed by an element that is not in the list, the property is migrated to a reference usage. |
| Refine | Dependency + Refinement MetadataIf refine is between a constraint and a requirement, it is migrated to a require constraint. |
| Initial Node | Start Node |
| Activity Final Node | Terminate Action Usage |
| Pseudo State (kind=initial) | Start Node |
| Activity Partition | Perform Action |
| Constraint Parameter | Attribute Usage with direction 'in' |
| Constraint | Textual Representation |
| Specification | Language and Body |
| Constraint Block | ConstraintDefinition / Calculation DefinitionCalculation Definition if the constraint block does not have an outgoing refine relationship to a requirement.Constraint Definition if the constraint block has an outgoing refine relation to a requirement. |
| Trace | Dependency |
| Note | Note |
| Allocate | Allocation Definition / Allocation Usage / DependencyAllocation Definition if both ends are migrated to "KerML::Classifier".Allocation Usage if both ends in the target model are "KerML::Feature".If both ends are not KerML::Type or they do not match by metatype (e.g., feature and classifier), it creates a Dependency. |
| TimeExpression | Payload feature value |
| TimeEvent | Trigger Invocation Expression (kind=at)If 'isRelative = true' then it is migrated to 'kind=after'. |
| ChangeEvent | Trigger Invocation Expression (kind=when) |
| Variable | Reference UsageIf the variable:Has no type: Reference usageTyped by data type: Attribute usageTyped by signal: Item usage Typed by class: Occurrence usageTyped by activity: Action usage Typed by a state machine: State usage Typed by use case: Use case usage Typed by interface: Port usageIf the variable is typed by an element that is not in the list, it is migrated to a reference usage. |
| Connection Point Reference | State Usage |
| PseudoState 'kind=entry' | State Usage |
| PseudoState 'kind=exit' | State Usage |
| Usage | Dependency |
| Realization | Dependency |
| Substitution | Dependency |
| Opaque Action | Action Usage |
| Element | Abstract |
| Profile | Model Library |
| Metaclass | Annotated Element |
| Opaque Behavior | Action Definition |
| Final State | Done state |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Use the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration plugin to enable model transformation features, allowing you to transform SysML v1 models to SysML v2. </span>The migration of SysML v1 models to SysML v2 is done by exporting a SysML v1 project to a local SysML v2 project with the file name '&lt;project_name&gt;_migrated'.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e77088b0-d4ef-4222-b4b9-b396c5d31898"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisite</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The SysML v1 to v2 Migration plugin, which is available in the Resource Manager via the <em>&lt;tool_installation_directory&gt;\data\resourcemanager\distribution\core.rdzip</em> file, must be <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Installation and system requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[installed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the modeling tool. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 style="text-align: left;">Migrating SysML v1 to v2</h3><p style="text-align: left;">To export a SysML v1 project to a local SysML v2 project file</p><hr /><ul><li>In the main menu, click<span> </span><strong>File<span> </span></strong>&gt;<span> </span><strong>Export To</strong><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span><strong>SysML v2 Model</strong>. Specify the project name and location in the<span> </span><strong>Save</strong><span> </span>window and click <strong>Save</strong>. The exported project is opened in the modeling tool. <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ad19732-2826-4843-9f3a-cd6da1069626"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Migration status</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If failures occur during the migration, a migration status file <em>MigrationReportOf&lt;projectName&gt;</em>.<em>xlsx</em> is generated. The link to the file is displayed in the Notification window. The file contains two sheets: <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">'Not Migrated Elements' and '</span>Not Migrated Element Properties', listing failed element and property migrations, respectively.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h3>SysML v1 to v2 element migration map</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c62e8f9f-2574-4b03-a40a-0b27e6509279"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">SysML v1 to v2 element migration - a work in progress</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="29e5cef0-ae03-4009-b003-ee8af449e117">Currently, only partial SysML v1 element migration is supported by the modeling tool, as SysML v1 to v2 element migration is a work in progress, covering over 80% of the SysML v1 to SysML v2 Transformation specification.</li><li data-uuid="1b8f7b89-b526-4e78-8961-e17396edb49d">Diagram migration currently supports only symbolic diagram to view usage migration. Tables, maps, matrices, etc., will be supported in future releases.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>See the table below for elements covered by the migration. </p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 48.2192%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 49.372%;" /><col style="width: 50.628%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: center;" scope="col">SysML v1 input</th><th style="text-align: center;">Result in the SysML v2 model</th></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Block <br /></span></td><td>Part Definition</td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Package</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Package</span></td></tr><tr><td><span ac:macro-id="6548b41d-ea78-4dcf-aefe-05aad1694059"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the property:</p><ul><li data-uuid="4ea34105-e83c-48ec-9118-3efe3e638ce8">Has no type: Reference usage</li><li data-uuid="d149dc00-935f-4e9e-b77c-c74e03673580">Typed by data type: Attribute usage</li><li data-uuid="6d7fbf4c-7c4e-4f25-b6a1-f6594faa5c50">Typed by signal: Item usage </li><li usage.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Refine</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Dependency + Refinement Metadata</p><ac:structured-macro constraint.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Node</p></td><td>Start Node</td></tr><tr><td><p>Activity Final Node</p></td><td>Terminate Action ac:macro-id="cb8440d5-67bf-4f6e-bebb-4ed3fab8e5e9"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="d32b54f8-aec8-4697-bfcc-596a1d933945">Calculation Definition if the constraint block does not have an outgoing requirement.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Trace</p></td><td>Dependency</td></tr><tr><td><p>Note</p></td><td>Note</td></tr><tr><td><p>Allocate</p></td><td><div ac:macro-id="bf6a8279-d585-4db6-bf42-7c5df6a2f1c2"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="ae994562-c6ca-4d07-a7b6-6da9998c645c">Allocation Definition if both ends are migrated to &quot;KerML::Classifier&quot;.</li><li data-uuid="1abd0189-c6e6-4913-af86-5533de8cba31">Allocation Usage if both ends in the target model are &quot;KerML::Feature&quot;.</li><li data-uuid="187c6e5c-e520-4984-b6ac-e2882bdd3068">If both ends are not KerML::Type or they do not match by metatype (e.g., feature and classifier), it creates a Dependency.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>TimeExpression</p></td><td>Payload feature value</td></tr><tr><td><p>TimeEvent</p></td><td><div ac:macro-id="78239483-7131-4e3e-b9e3-685b7f628ab7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If 'isRelative = true' then it is migrated to 'kind=after'.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>ChangeEvent</p></td><td>Trigger Invocation Expression (kind=when)</td></tr><tr><td><p>Variable</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Reference Usage</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54dc2d33-c702-4f58-83df-7cd307800ecb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the variable:</p><ul><li data-uuid="c53f05e4-441d-4392-9b92-c3a1ec60eb8f">Has no type: Reference usage</li><li data-uuid="ad535b19-de10-4a4b-9ec8-720ac5979bbf">Typed by data type: Attribute usage</li><li <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006450 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00155: (2026x Refresh1) Port

- page_id: `304006450`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006450/2026x+Refresh1+Port
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:29.989+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A port is a connection point through which a part definition makes some of its features available in a limited way. A port definition defines features that can be made available via ports. Two ports are compatible for connection if they have directed features that match with inverse directions, which can be established using port conjugation.

##### Creating port usage and definition elements

###### Creating port usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create port usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *port def*for a port definition.
  2. port for a port usage.
2. Specify the element name.
3. (Optional) Specify the port features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

portdefOnOffCmdPort{outonOffCmd:OnOffCmd;}portonOffCmdPort:OnOffCmdPort;

###### Creating port usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create port usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. port for port usage.
  2. port def for port definition.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the port's features.

###### Creating port usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create port usage and definition elements via the view palette

- To create an interface definition:
  1. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click the **port** def button to create an interface definition element.
  2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the port definition symbol.
- To create an interface usage:
  1. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click the **port** button to create an interface usage.
  2. In a view, click on an element symbol for which you want to create a port. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **port**or the **port****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.

###### Creating port usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To create port usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the port command. A port is created for the element.

###### Creating port usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''] page.

To create port usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select an element symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the port element.
  - Select an element symbol and on the right side of the ports compartment, click the Create port button. The new element is created and displayed in the ports compartment.

###### Creating port usage elements via the Specification panel

To create port usage elements via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **port**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **ports**compartment section's bar, click the **Create port**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element is created in the **ports** compartment.

###### Auto-creating port definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create a port definition element via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

##### Conjugating ports

Every port definition has an implicit conjugate port definition that reverses input and output features. You can easily use the conjugate port definitions by prepending the ~ symbol to the port definition name.

###### Using conjugated ports definitions via the Textual Editor

To use conjugate port definitions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want the element to be created and declare the port keyword.
2. (Optional) Follow with the port name.
3. Follow with the feature typing relationship.
4. Specify the conjugated port definition:
  1. Full conjugation notation:
    1. Port definition name followed by :: (qualified name notation), followed by the port definition name with the ~ symbol prepended.
  2. Shorthand notation:
    1. Port definition name with the ~ symbol prepended.
5. Click the Synchronize button.

portdefFuelPort{ // Port definition 
attributetemperature:Temp; 
outitemfuelSupply:Fuel; 
initemfuelReturn:Fuel; 
} 
partdefFuelTankAssembly{ 
portfuelTankPort:FuelPort; 
} 
partdefEngine{ 
 port engineFuelPort : FuelPort::'~FuelPort'; //Conjugate port definition 
portengineFuelPort: ~FuelPort; // Using a ~ symbol on the port type is a shorthand for using the conjugate port definition 
}

##### Directed port features

A feature with a direction (in, out or inout) is a directed feature. Two ports are compatible for connection if they have directed features that match with inverse directions.

###### Creating directed port features

To create directed port features

1. In the Textual Editor, prepend the direction keyword - in , out , inout - to the feature.
2. Click the Synchronize button. portdefFuelOutPort{ 
attributetemperature:Temp; 
outitemfuelSupply:Fuel;// feature fuelSupply with direction out 
initemfuelReturn:Fuel;// feature fuelReturn with compatible direction in 
} 
portdefFuelInPort{ 
attributetemperature:Temp; 
initemfuelSupply:Fuel;// feature fuelSupply with compatible direction in 
outitemfuelReturn:Fuel;// feature fuelReturn with compatible direction out 
}

The shortcut menu appears. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>port </strong>element in the shortcut menu.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>ports </strong>compartment section's bar, click the <strong>Create port </strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The element is created in the <strong>ports</strong> compartment.</span></li></ol></li></ol><h4>Auto-creating port definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</h4><p><span>To auto-create a port definition element via the Extract Definition commands</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage" /></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Conjugating ports</h3><p>Every port definition has an implicit conjugate port definition that reverses input and output features. You can easily use the conjugate port definitions by prepending the ~ symbol to the port definition name. </p><h4>Using conjugated ports definitions via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To use conjugate port definitions via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want the element to be created and declare the <em>port</em> keyword.</li><li>(Optional) Follow with the port name.</li><li>Follow with the feature typing relationship.</li><li>Specify the conjugated port definition:<ol><li>Full conjugation notation: <ol><li>Port definition name followed by :: (qualified name notation), followed by the port definition name with the ~ symbol prepended.</li></ol></li><li>Shorthand notation:<ol><li>Port definition name with the ~ symbol prepended.</li></ol></li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f352e50-2d26-4ad2-9d11-50ce9277573f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {                                                         <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Port definition</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">temperature</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Temp</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">out</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelSupply</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Fuel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelReturn</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Fuel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelTankAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTankPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">port</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engineFuelPort</span> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelPort::</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'~FuelPort'</span>;              <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">Conjugate port definition</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engineFuelPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : ~</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                               <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Using a ~ symbol on the port type is a shorthand for using the conjugate port definition</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Directed port features</h3><p>A feature with a direction (in, out or inout) is a directed feature. Two ports are compatible for connection if they have directed features that match with inverse directions.</p><h4>Creating directed port features</h4><p>To create directed port features</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, prepend the direction keyword - <em>in</em>, <em>out</em>, <em>inout</em> - to the feature.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="77ae2334-7366-4b0a-877c-e7480662bac9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelOutPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span fuelSupply with direction out </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314181539 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00156: (2026x Refresh1) Post-processing the SysML v2 model

- page_id: `314181539`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314181539/2026x+Refresh1+Post-processing+the+SysML+v2+model
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-06-25T11:42:50.900+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 to SysML v2 transition > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 to v2 model conversion
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

##### Overview

This activity diagram describes activities performed after the initial transformation has completed.

The objective of post-processing is to adapt the generated SysML v2 model to organizational modeling practices and fully exploit SysML v2 capabilities.

##### Process

The process starts with evaluating the generated SysML v2 model.

Systems engineers then perform a series of manual improvement activities:

- Reorganize the model structure.
- Refactor model elements.
- Apply SysML v2 modeling practices.
- Ensure compliance with organizational modeling methods.

Examples of post-processing activities include introducing usage-focused modeling patterns, restructuring model ownership hierarchies, and aligning the model with organizational conventions.

The process is iterative. If the model does not satisfy organizational requirements, additional improvements are applied until compliance is achieved.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Post-processing the SysML v2 model.jpg" /></ac:image></strong></p><h3>Overview</h3><p>This activity diagram describes activities performed after the initial transformation has completed.</p><p>The objective of post-processing is to adapt the generated SysML v2 model to organizational modeling practices and fully exploit SysML v2 capabilities.</p><h3>Process </h3><p>The process starts with evaluating the generated SysML v2 model.</p><p>Systems engineers then perform a series of manual improvement activities:</p><ul><li data-uuid="ed4d1b7e-de11-4488-8c7e-401a41e3c17a">Reorganize the model structure.</li><li data-uuid="3dad46b9-9f3d-449f-b048-f1ff5ced5566">Refactor model elements.</li><li data-uuid="db4b75eb-bf7d-4237-a8f5-1694cad506eb">Apply SysML v2 modeling practices.</li><li data-uuid="f3b90b48-afcb-4341-b6d6-70e5801307e9">Ensure compliance with organizational modeling methods.</li></ul><p>Examples of post-processing activities include introducing usage-focused modeling patterns, restructuring model ownership hierarchies, and aligning the model with organizational conventions.</p><p>The process is iterative. If the model does not satisfy organizational requirements, additional improvements are applied until compliance is achieved.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314181525 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00157: (2026x Refresh1) Pre-processing the SysML v1 model

- page_id: `314181525`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314181525/2026x+Refresh1+Pre-processing+the+SysML+v1+model
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-06-25T11:42:15.548+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 to SysML v2 transition > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 to v2 model conversion
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

##### Overview

This activity diagram describes the activities required to prepare a SysML v1 model before transformation.

The objective of this phase is to improve model quality, reduce migration risks, and ensure that the model is suitable for conversion to SysML v2.

##### Process

The preprocessing workflow starts by understanding the capabilities and limitations of the selected transformation tooling.

Depending on the migration strategy, the process follows one of two paths:

- One-off Migration

The source model is prepared and migrated once. Future development continues in the SysML v2 model.

- Incremental Migration

The source model continues to evolve. Projects may be partitioned into smaller subprojects and migrated incrementally. Subsequent migrations evaluate model changes and introduce additional subprojects when necessary.

##### Model Preparation Activities

The following activities are performed before transformation:

- Remove obsolete and no longer maintained model content.
- Apply organizational modeling conventions.
- Review element naming.
- Execute SysML v1 conformance checks.
- Minimize ambiguities and redundancies.
- Identify tool-specific extensions.
- Remove unsupported tool-specific extensions.
- Retain required stereotypes and profiles.

Particular attention should be given to element naming. While unnamed elements are common in SysML v1 models, SysML v2 textual notation relies heavily on explicitly named elements. Therefore, reviewing and improving naming consistency is considered a critical preparation activity.

Profiles and stereotypes intended for migration should be redesigned as SysML v2 libraries and metadata definitions and made available within the target SysML v2 environment.

The preprocessing phase concludes by identifying unsupported features and ensuring that appropriate handling strategies are defined before transformation begins.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Pre-processing the SysML v1 model.jpg" /></ac:image></strong></p><h3>Overview</h3><p>This activity diagram describes the activities required to prepare a SysML v1 model before transformation.</p><p>The objective of this phase is to improve model quality, reduce migration risks, and ensure that the model is suitable for conversion to SysML v2.</p><h3>Process </h3><p>The preprocessing workflow starts by understanding the capabilities and limitations of the selected transformation tooling.</p><p>Depending on the migration strategy, the process follows one of two paths:</p><ul><li data-uuid="5d208b39-6be7-4c4e-908f-71cf0849b2fe">One-off Migration</li></ul><p>The source model is prepared and migrated once. Future development continues in the SysML v2 model.</p><ul><li data-uuid="9250e489-f8a3-4ead-99a3-01b536d255ed">Incremental Migration</li></ul><p>The source model continues to evolve. Projects may be partitioned into smaller subprojects and migrated incrementally. Subsequent migrations evaluate model changes and introduce additional subprojects when necessary.</p><h3>Model Preparation Activities</h3><p>The following activities are performed before transformation:</p><ul><li data-uuid="d5b7dda6-1c8a-4b8b-bb95-ae16f3c5a46a">Remove obsolete and no longer maintained model content.</li><li data-uuid="0b887959-be84-4247-ad98-030ad07dafd1">Apply organizational modeling conventions.</li><li data-uuid="08a9495d-a265-431c-aed3-570e028966e6">Review element naming.</li><li data-uuid="3e4ec9a1-5f53-4233-acd4-d04f6ef1f8da">Execute SysML v1 conformance checks.</li><li data-uuid="566dcc07-ca72-43c1-885e-dfe7e55d78e1">Minimize ambiguities and redundancies.</li><li data-uuid="fcd623c4-4b42-4c0d-90e1-7659b30f7f7f">Identify tool-specific extensions.</li><li data-uuid="12e0dc3c-efd3-4382-9413-4dc71ab3986e">Remove unsupported tool-specific extensions.</li><li data-uuid="e5b23b67-679e-4ac1-8d5b-31c90c386ded">Retain required stereotypes and profiles.</li></ul><p>Particular attention should be given to element naming. While unnamed elements are common in SysML v1 models, SysML v2 textual notation relies heavily on explicitly named elements. Therefore, reviewing and improving naming consistency is considered a critical preparation activity.</p><p>Profiles and stereotypes intended for migration should be redesigned as SysML v2 libraries and metadata definitions and made available within the target SysML v2 environment.</p><p>The preprocessing phase concludes by identifying unsupported features and ensuring that appropriate handling strategies are defined before transformation begins.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005889 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00158: (2026x Refresh1) Predefined style sheets

- page_id: `304005889`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005889/2026x+Refresh1+Predefined+style+sheets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:49.984+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Each SysML v2 project is automatically loaded with the *3DS**SysML Customization* library containing predefined style sheets. Each style sheet contains symbol style rules based on the element metaclasses.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Symbol styles represented by the *DefaultStyleSheet*(left)**and *BlackAndWhiteStyle*Sheet**(right) style sheets.

There are two predefined style sheets in the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets*package:

50%

- DefaultStyleSheet The DefaultStyleSheet style sheet contains rule-based styles that apply to all element symbols in SysML v2 projects by default based on the element metaclass.
  - You can view the predefined style definitions used for symbol styles in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Style sheet styles tables' space=''] and the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions*package. [ATTACHMENT filename='defaultstylesheetpartdefinition.png']

50%

- BlackAndWhiteStyleSheet The BlackAndWhiteStyleSheet style sheet contains rule-based styles for representing all element symbols in a black-and-white style. [ATTACHMENT filename='blackandwhitestylesheetpartdef.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Each style sheet contains symbol style rules based on the element metaclasses.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="beec9354-774a-49b4-b1ce-d2989f9db4f9"><em>DefaultStyleSheet<br metaclass.<ul style="text-align: left;"><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">You can view the predefined style definitions used for symbol styles in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="8c1ee87b-25bd-4f79-bf3e-e335e39282aa"><em>BlackAndWhiteStyleSheet</em><em><br /></em>The <em>BlackAndWhiteStyleSheet </em>style - version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:14.224+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Validation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The validation rules are bundled into sets called validation suites. The modeling tool contains the following predefined validation suites, each with its own set of validation rules:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysMLConstraintsSuite' space='']. The suite contains SysML v2 and KerML validation rules.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) DassaultSystemesValidationSuite' space='']. The suite contains custom Dassault Systèmes validation rules, supplementing the SysML v2 and KerML validation rules.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The model is only validated against enabled validation suites. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Enabling validation suites' space=''] page.
- In addition to using the predefined validation suites, you can model your own validation suites with custom validation rules. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom validation suites and rules' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The modeling tool contains the following predefined validation suites, each with its own set of validation rules:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="553d01c4-dc35-4394-aa93-655d87a0cb56"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysMLConstraintsSuite" /></ac:link>. The suite contains SysML v2 and KerML validation rules.</span></li><li data-uuid="3b93ddf3-28a4-40d1-9877-614ecd7672f5"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) DassaultSystemesValidationSuite" /></ac:link>. The suite contains custom Dassault Systèmes validation rules, supplementing the SysML v2 and KerML validation rules.</span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Predefined_validation_suites.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Predefined validation suites" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e726f7a1-1bf8-4b53-8226-9a87b2f67ad8"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="322815fa-625f-44c1-8195-47da36cb9c05"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The model is only validated against enabled validation suites. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314181517 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00160: (2026x Refresh1) Preparing the organization for transition

- page_id: `314181517`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314181517/2026x+Refresh1+Preparing+the+organization+for+transition
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-06-25T11:41:55.501+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 to SysML v2 transition > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 to v2 model conversion
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

##### Overview

This activity diagram describes the activities required to prepare an organization for the adoption of SysML v2.

The process involves multiple stakeholder groups including systems engineers, IT engineers, software developers, and an interdisciplinary cross-functional transition team.

##### Process

The preparation phase begins with establishing a transition team responsible for defining the overall migration strategy and transition plans. These plans typically address migration goals, schedules, budgets, pilot projects, training activities, and methodology evolution.

Pilot projects are then conducted to assess the impact of SysML v2 on existing MBSE practices. Typical pilot objectives include:

- Increasing organizational awareness of SysML v2.
- Building initial SysML v2 expertise.
- Evaluating candidate SysML v1 models for migration.
- Assessing available SysML v2 tooling.
- Establishing migration approaches and practices.
- Evaluating integrations, plugins, and reporting solutions.
- Defining metrics for tracking migration progress.

Systems engineers evaluate and select SysML v2 modeling tools, while IT engineers deploy and configure the required modeling infrastructure and model repositories.

In parallel, software developers adapt existing tool customizations and integrations to operate in a SysML v2 environment. This frequently includes updates to:

- Plugins.
- Reports.
- Scripts.
- External integrations.

After the infrastructure is established, systems engineers update organizational methodologies, training materials, community resources, reference architectures, and reusable assets to support SysML v2.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

These plans typically address migration goals, schedules, budgets, pilot projects, training activities, and methodology evolution.</p><p>Pilot projects are then conducted to assess the impact of SysML v2 on existing MBSE practices. Typical pilot objectives include:</p><ul><li data-uuid="88a33fd9-2c01-4fe3-9771-d9d224246a18">Increasing organizational awareness of SysML v2.</li><li data-uuid="5688ac4c-dcbc-4792-b9a3-f8c9412a2c5c">Building initial SysML v2 expertise.</li><li data-uuid="93c3bc14-b48e-4b51-a5f3-de1b69bb32cd">Evaluating candidate SysML v1 models for migration.</li><li data-uuid="3a0f4c30-3385-4fd0-9f94-c69a324901c8">Assessing available SysML v2 tooling.</li><li data-uuid="f161ca3f-5165-486f-a9de-fbebc81bca89">Establishing migration approaches and practices.</li><li data-uuid="52146c88-7ebf-439f-98a1-2c56cfbef44f">Evaluating integrations, plugins, and reporting solutions.</li><li data-uuid="7375bca3-bdb9-4a62-a846-88411777b682">Defining metrics for tracking migration progress.</li></ul><p>Systems engineers evaluate and select SysML v2 modeling tools, while IT engineers deploy and configure the required modeling infrastructure and model repositories.</p><p>In parallel, software developers adapt existing tool customizations and integrations to operate in a SysML v2 environment. This frequently includes updates to:</p><ul><li data-uuid="bafc7d52-8d9f-4444-8fe7-89a1eaa866fc">Plugins.</li><li data-uuid="6c72ab79-21ad-4540-8765-f9c982f60d9a">Reports.</li><li data-uuid="166be5fb-fc3d-49a5-9764-a9a3dc9c142f">Scripts.</li><li data-uuid="9de9e6d3-35b5-4312-9ff7-f8ff19811764">External integrations.</li></ul><p>After the infrastructure is established, systems engineers update organizational methodologies, training materials, community resources, reference architectures, and reusable assets to support SysML v2.</p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005787 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00161: (2026x Refresh1) Prerequisites

- page_id: `304005787`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005787/2026x+Refresh1+Prerequisites
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-06-25T11:45:40.873+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To work with all of the SysML v2 features, the following software should be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Installation and system requirements' space='']:

- One of the following CATIA Magic/No Magic modeling tools:
  - Cameo Systems Modeler (Enterprise Edition only)
  - Cameo Enterprise Architecture
  - Magic Cyber Systems Engineer
  - Magic Systems of Systems Architect
- SysML v1 Plugin and Cameo Requirements Modeler. The plugins must be installed in addition to the following plugins to use SysML v2 in the modeling tool.
- SysML v2 Plugin. It provides the modeling tool with the majority of the SysML v2 features. It also includes the SysML v2 Textual Editor, allowing you to work with the SysML v2 features using textual notation via a textual editor.
- (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin Documentation' space='SYSML2EP']. It allows you to conduct static SysML v2 model evaluations (mathematical calculations, calculation usage evaluation, constraints evaluation, and requirements evaluation).
- (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Performing SysML v1 to v2 model transformation' space='']. It enables model transformation from SysML v1 to SysML v2 features.
- (Optional) SysML v2 Simulation Plugin . It enables execution of SysML v2 models (state machine simulations, mathematical calculations, and requirements verification).

For the SysML v2 Open API documentation, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML v2' space='DEVG'] page in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home' space='DEVG']documentation.

#### NOTE: Font Compatibility on macOS

Font Compatibility on macOS

The default font used in the modeling tool for SysML v2 is Roboto. However, because Roboto is not pre-installed on macOS, certain non-Latin characters may not render correctly when using the tool on macOS. To avoid character rendering issues, please [download](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto) and [install](https://support.apple.com/guide/font-book/install-and-validate-fonts-fntbk1000/mac) the Roboto font on your machine.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006153 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00162: (2026x Refresh1) Printing

- page_id: `304006153`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006153/2026x+Refresh1+Printing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:07.120+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with views > (2026x Refresh1) View image export
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can print:

- Active diagram
- Any diagram(s)
- All open diagrams
- Selected symbols of active diagram

You can specify the printing options or preview pages by using the following dialogs:

- Print Dialog
- Print Options Dialog
- Print Preview Window

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Print dialog

##### Printing

###### Printing an active diagram

To print an active diagram

1. Open a diagram you want to print.
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the main toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='print.png'] Print Active Diagram button.
  - In the main menu, click File > Print or press Ctrl+P on the keyboard . On the Print dialog, Print what area, select Active diagram , and click the Print button.

###### Printing any diagram(s)

To print any diagram(s)

1. In the main menu, click File > Print or press Ctrl+P on the keyboard . The Print dialog opens.
2. In the Print what area, choose Selected diagram(s) . The Select Diagram dialog opens.
3. Select the diagram(s) you want to print.
4. Click OK .
5. Click the Print button.

###### Printing all open diagrams

To print all open diagrams

1. In the main menu, click File > Print or press Ctrl+P on the keyboard . The Print dialog opens.
2. In the Print what area, select All opened diagram(s) .
3. Click the Print button.

###### Printing selected symbols of active diagram

To print selected symbols of active diagram

1. On the diagram, select the symbols you want to print.
2. In the main menu, click File > Print or press Ctrl+P on the keyboard. The Print dialog opens.
3. In the Print dialog, Print what area, choose Selected symbols of active diagram
4. Click the Print button.

##### Print preview

You can preview diagrams before printing them using the **Print Preview**window.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Print Preview window

###### Previewing diagrams before printing

To preview diagrams before printing

1. Open the Print Preview window in one of the following ways:
  1. In the main toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='print_preview_button.png'] Print Preview button.
  2. In the main menu, click File > Print Preview .
2. Select the diagram you want to preview from the list on the left side of the window. The diagram is previewed on the right side of the window.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The diagram is previewed on the right side of the window. </li></ol><p><br /></p>
