## PAGE 00164: (2026x Refresh1) Project options

- page_id: `304006172`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006172/2026x+Refresh1+Project+options
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-05-06T12:22:28.316+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Project management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the**Project Options**dialog to specify general project-specific options.The**Project Options**dialog includes option groups, each designated for specific features and containing lists of corresponding options. Groups are represented using the tree structure.

##### Opening the Project Options dialog

To open the Project Options dialog

- In the main menu, click Options > Project . [ATTACHMENT filename='project_options_dialog.png']

##### Modifying project options

To modify project options

1. Open the **Project Options** dialog .
2. Locate the option you want to modify. Use the quick filter to find the needed option in the list. Filtering is performed in all available options, not only the selected option group. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']Click the **Show Description** button to display the project option description section at the bottom of the dialog to read about the option functionality. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. An option value can be changed by typing a new value, setting a value to true / false , or selecting a value from the list. Click the **Reset to Defaults** button to restore default option values.
4. Click OK .

##### Project Options

The table below displays the default project options. Additional options may appear based on installed plugins.

| Option | Default value | Value options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| General |  |  |  |
| Diagrams |  |  |  |
| Image Export |  |  |  |
| Time Limit to Build Diagram | 60 | Number | Specify the maximum time (in seconds) allotted for diagram building while exporting a diagram. If the time limit is exceeded, the diagram is not exported. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the time limit. >]]> |
| Image Height Limit | 10000 | Number | Specify the maximum image height (in pixels) for diagram export. If the height limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image height limit. >]]> |
| Image Width Limit | 10000 | Number | Specify the maximum image width (in pixels) for diagram export. If the width limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image width limit. >]]> |
| Export Preference When Limits Exceeded | Export Cropped Diagram | Export Cropped DiagramDo Not Export | Specify the diagram export preference if the diagram image height and/or width limits are exceeded. >]]> |
| General |  |  |  |
| General |  |  |  |
| Path to Used Project | <project.dir><install.root>\profiles<install.root>\modelLibraries | Custom | Specify the paths to store used projects and extension elements. |
| Decimal Places | 4 | Number | Specify the maximum number of decimal places to be used when displaying the real number in tables and element Specification windows. |
| SysML v2 |  |  |  |
| SysML v2 |  |  |  |
| Active Relationship Creation Dialog | SysMLRelationshipCreationDialog | Custom | Specify the Active Create Relationship Dialog configuration. >]]> |
| Active Element Creation Dialog | SysMLElementCreationDialog | Custom | Specify the Active Create Element Dialog configuration. >]]> |
| Active View Creation Dialog | SysMLViewCreationDialog | Custom | Specify the Active Create View Dialog configuration. >]]> |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Use the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Project Options</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> dialog to s</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">pecify general project-specific options. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Project Options</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> dialog includes option groups, each designated for specific features and containing lists of corresponding options. Groups are represented using the tree structure.</span></p><h3>Opening the Project Options dialog</h3><p>To open the Project Options dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_options_dialog.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Modifying project options</h3><p>To modify project options</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Options dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Locate the option you want to modify.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7672be49-876c-41ce-9a52-f49c111fe1d2"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Use the quick filter to find the needed option in the list. Filtering is performed in all available options, not only the selected option group.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_options_quick_filter.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click the <strong>Show Description</strong> button to display the project option description section at the bottom of the dialog to read about the option functionality.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="The Show Description button in the Project Options toolbar"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_group_toolbar.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>An option value can be changed by typing a new value, setting a value to <em>true</em>/<em>false</em>, or selecting a value from the list.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37f945ab-1085-4e2c-9a06-d8bd3009f2a3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Click the <strong>Reset to Defaults</strong> button to restore default option values.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Project Options</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="894362a7-7ba8-4d42-9b4e-d7a987937a9d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The table below displays the default project options. Additional options may appear based on installed plugins.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 102.448%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 15.8723%;" /><col style="width: 17.7904%;" /><col style="width: 10.3098%;" /><col style="width: 56.0085%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Option</th><th>Default value</th><th>Value options</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>General</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" title="Background color : Light blue 35%" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Diagrams</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" title="Background color : Light grey 100%" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Image Export</td></tr><tr><td>Time Limit to Build Diagram </td><td>60</td><td>Number</td><td>Specify the maximum time (in seconds) allotted for diagram building while exporting a diagram. If the time limit is exceeded, the diagram is not exported. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the time limit. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) View image export" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td>Image Height Limit</td><td>10000</td><td>Number</td><td>Specify the maximum image height (in pixels) for diagram export. If the height limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image height limit. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) View image export" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td>Image Width Limit</td><td>10000</td><td>Number</td><td>Specify the maximum image width (in pixels) for diagram export. If the width limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image width limit. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) View image export" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td>Export Preference When Limits Exceeded</td><td>Export Cropped Diagram</td><td><p>Export Cropped Diagram</p><p>Do Not Export</p></td><td>Specify the diagram export preference if the diagram image height and/or width limits are exceeded. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) View image export" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" title="Background color : Light blue 35%" colspan="4" Project</td><td><p>&lt;project.dir&gt;</p><p>&lt;install.root&gt;\profiles</p><p>&lt;install.root&gt;\modelLibraries</p></td><td>Custom</td><td>Specify the paths to store used projects and extension elements.</td></tr><tr><td>Decimal Places</td><td>4</td><td>Number</td><td>Specify the maximum number of decimal places to be used when displaying the real number in tables and element Specification windows.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>SysML v2</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" colspan="4" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006212 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00165: (2026x Refresh1) Project Statistics

- page_id: `304006212`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006212/2026x+Refresh1+Project+Statistics
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:11.479+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Project management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The project statistics feature calculates detailed statistics for the entire project and exports the results to a separate file. The exported statistical report will help solve performance problems you may be experiencing. Project statistics are calculated in the main project and, if selected, in used projects. The generated detailed project statistics file includes quantitative data about diagrams, elements, Project/Environment options, hardware & system, Teamwork Cloud projects, and performance.

#### NOTE: Confidential Information

Confidential Information

The exported file contains the names of the main project, used projects (if selected), and used elements. If they are confidential, you should select **Confidential**to keep their names hidden.

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Exporting detailed statistics

To export detailed statistics

1. In the main menu, select Tools > Project Statistics .
2. In the Project Statistics dialog, click the Export Detailed Statistics button. The Export Detailed Statistics Options dialog opens.
3. Select data to export along with the OS and hardware, diagrams/elements by types, and project options statistics that are included by default.
4. Specify other export options:
  - Select Include Used Project Statistics to generate and export used project statistics as well.
  - Select Confidential to keep the project element names hidden in the exported statistics report.
5. Click Export . The Select Location dialog opens.
6. Select the location where the file should be exported and click Save .
7. When the statistics file is generated, click Close .

##### The project statistics file

The generated detailed project statistics file includes the following quantitative data:

**Teamwork Cloud projects**

- Teamwork Cloud project local cache size on disk
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache latest blob size
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob size by revision
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob count by revision
- Teamwork Cloud project history depth
- Teamwork Cloud local project binary cache size
- Teamwork Cloud local project cache dir size
- Root object count by type
- Used projects count

**OS & Hardware**

- Hardware (RAM, OS, disk size and type) and Java info (heap)
- Restricted dir size

**Diagrams**

- Total size of diagrams binaries
- Diagram count by type
- Diagram count by kind

**Elements**

- Total element count
- Total size of strings
- Elements by meta-type
- Features by size
- String features by string length
- KerML based Elements statistics
  - Non-Standard Library Elements
  - Standard Library Elements

**Metadata**

- Diagram statistics
- Element statistics

**Standard Libraries**

- Diagram statistics
- Element statistics

**Symbol Styles**

- Diagram statistics
- Element statistics

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The project statistics feature calculates detailed statistics for the entire project and exports the results to a separate file. The exported statistical report will help solve performance problems you may be experiencing. Project statistics are calculated in the main project and, if selected, in used projects. The generated detailed project statistics file includes quantitative data about diagrams, elements, Project/Environment options, hardware &amp; system, Teamwork Cloud projects, and performance.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="811f00dd-6f47-4a6a-99ee-daa68a80650c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Confidential Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The exported file contains the names of the main project, used projects (if selected), and used elements. If they are confidential, you should select <strong>Confidential </strong>to keep their names hidden.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_statistics_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project Statistics" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h3><h3>Exporting detailed statistics</h3><p>To export detailed statistics</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Tools </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Statistics</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Statistics</strong> dialog, click the <strong>Export Detailed Statistics</strong> button. The <strong>Export Detailed Statistics Options </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Select data to export along with the OS and hardware, diagrams/elements by types, and project options statistics that are included by default.</li><li>Specify other export options:<ul><li>Select <strong>Include Used Project Statistics</strong> to generate and export used project statistics as well.</li><li>Select <strong>Confidential </strong>to keep the project element names hidden in the exported statistics report. </li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Export</strong>. The <strong>Select Location </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Select the location where the file should be exported and click <strong>Save</strong>.</li><li>When the statistics file is generated, click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>The project statistics file</h3><p>The generated detailed project statistics file includes the following quantitative data:</p><p><strong>Teamwork Cloud projects</strong></p><ul><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache size on disk</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache latest blob size</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob size by revision</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob count by revision</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project history depth</li><li>Teamwork Cloud local project binary cache size</li><li>Teamwork Cloud local project cache dir size</li><li>Root object count by type</li><li>Used projects count</li></ul><p><strong>OS &amp; Hardware </strong></p><ul><li>Hardware (RAM, OS, disk size and type) and Java info (heap)</li><li>Restricted dir size</li></ul><p><strong>Diagrams</strong></p><ul><li>Total size of diagrams binaries</li><li>Diagram count by type </li><li>Diagram count by kind</li></ul><p><strong>Elements</strong></p><ul><li>Total element count</li><li>Total size of strings</li><li>Elements by meta-type</li><li>Features by size</li><li>String features by string length</li><li>KerML based Elements statistics<ul><li>Non-Standard Library Elements</li><li>Standard Library Elements</li></ul></li></ul><p><strong>Metadata</strong></p><ul><li>Diagram statistics</li><li>Element statistics</li></ul><p><strong>Standard Libraries</strong></p><ul><li>Diagram statistics</li><li>Element statistics</li></ul><p><strong>Symbol Styles</strong></p><ul><li>Diagram statistics</li><li>Element statistics</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006178 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00166: (2026x Refresh1) Quick find

- page_id: `304006178`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006178/2026x+Refresh1+Quick+find
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:09.329+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Project management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the Quick Find functionality to search for specific elements in the model. The simple and easy-to-use dialog is designed to locate the needed element by its declared or short name.

##### Opening the Quick Find dialog

To open the Quick Find dialog, do one of the following

- On the Containment tab toolbar click the [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_find_button.png'] Quick Find button. **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**
- On the main menu, click Edit > Quick Find .
- On the keyboard, press Ctrl+Alt+F or Cmd+Opt+F .

Search results are displayed in the drop-down list, which opens when you start typing the search phrase. Click the element you need in this list or correct the search query to perform a new search. The element will be selected in the Containment tree and on the active view if the element has symbols on the active view.

##### Quick Find dialog elements

The following table provides descriptions of the **Quick Find** dialog elements.

| Dialog element name/image | Dialog element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Search for | Options group | Click Any Element to search in all elements of your project.Click View to search only in views. |
| Search By Name | Text box with a drop-down list | Type a phrase to search for in element names. See the search results in the drop-down list.The following elements are excluded from the search results: (e.g., in, abstract, derived, etc.)Element metaclasses (e.g., part, attribute, package)Feature values (owned expressions) (except for Association and Connector)Expressions (except for ConstraintUsage and CalculationUsage)Features owned by MetadataFeature (e.g., Documentation, Comment, TextualRepresentation)Special actions (e.g., ControlNodes, AssignActionUsage, SendActionUsage)Features owned by special actions (parameters) |
|  | Menu button | Click the button and customize search options. Match Text AnywhereTo search for matching phrase in any part of the element name, click the command to add the check mark.To explicitly search from the beginning of element name, click the command to remove the check mark.Use Camel CaseTo search for matching phrase typed in camel case, click the command to add the check mark.To search for matching phrase typed in regular case, click the command to remove the check mark.You can use camel case as follows:Type only the first letters (either capitalized or not) of each word, for example, "and" or "AND" to find "Analysis and Design".Type skipping spacers, for example, "cv3" to find "CV-3". |
| More | Menu button | Click the button to load the next 100 matches. By default, the first 100 matches are shown in the dialog. |
| Expand | Menu button | Click the button to load all model elements. |
| TreeList | Tabs | Click to switch from the Tree view to the List, and vice versa. |
| Previous Next | Menu button | Click to navigate to a previous or next result.Available in the Tree view only. |
| Apply Filter (Ctrl+Space) | Check box | Click to clear the check box, if you need to remove the search scope filter. If you clear the Apply Filter check box once, your selection is remembered for the next time. |
| Filter Options | Menu button | Click the button and customize the search scope filter options. Ensure the Apply Filter check box is selectedHide Standard/System LibrariesReduces the search results list by hiding elements that belong to the Standard/System Libraries. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005601 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00167: (2026x Refresh1) Quick Properties Panel

- page_id: `304005601`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005601/2026x+Refresh1+Quick+Properties+Panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:39.851+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Project management > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling tool environment > (2026x Refresh1) User interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

TheQuick properties panelhas two tabs, as shown in the figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Quick properties panel

- The**Zoom**tab zooms in on the current view.
- The**Change Sets**tab shows the latest changes compared against the most recent project version saved in the local repository

##### Zoom tab

The Zoom tab allows you to preview and zoom in or out of the active view.

###### Opening the Zoom tab

To open the Zoom tab

- Do one of the following:
  - In the Model Browser, click the Zoom tab.
  - In the main menu, click Window > Zoom .

###### Displaying a view in the Zoom tab

To display a view in the Zoom tab

- Do one of the following:
  - Click the view in the Containment tree.
  - Click the view tab.

###### Zooming in or out of the active view

To zoom in or out of the active view

- In the Zoom tab, drag the corners of the blue square.

###### Quickly accessing any part of the view

To quickly access any part of the view

- In the Zoom tab, drag the blue square to the desired part of the view. The selected view part will be displayed in the view pane.

###### Fitting the view to the window

To fit the view to the window

- In the Zoom tab, on the blue square [ATTACHMENT filename='fit_window_button.png'] , click the Fit in Window button or press CTRL+W .

##### Change Sets tab

Use the Change Sets tab to track project modifications. The latest changes are compared against the last saved state (via **Save Project** or **Ctrl+S**commands) of the project in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects' space=''].

- The panel legend explains the modification markings.
- Click the Refresh icon to update the display.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The<span> </span>Quick properties panel<span> </span>has two tabs, as shown in the figure below.</span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Quick Properties Panel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Quick Properties Panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Quick properties panel<br /></span></h6><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="46fd7003-a8ed-4568-ad0d-9e8c4ab6be83"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The<strong><span> </span>Zoom</strong><span> </span>tab zooms in on the current view.</span></li><li data-uuid="c78a0d05-2041-48e5-b870-ba338c539b38"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The<strong><span> </span>Change Sets</strong><span> </span>tab shows the latest changes compared against the most recent project version saved in the local repository</span><br /></span></li></ul><h3>Zoom tab</h3><p>The Zoom tab allows you to preview and zoom in or out of the active view.</p><h4>Opening the Zoom tab</h4><p>To open the Zoom tab</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="6cf87d6d-7eb0-4d62-b46d-132f671b127b">Do one of the following:<ul><li data-uuid="2c019ebd-4651-4261-95ac-5174af036d50">In the Model Browser, click the <strong>Zoom</strong> tab.</li><li data-uuid="2883cdd8-740d-4568-a3f8-c5dc5916d0bc">In the main menu, click <strong>Window</strong> &gt; <strong>Zoom</strong>.</li></ul></li></ul><h4>Displaying a view in the Zoom tab</h4><p>To display a view in the Zoom tab</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="c6496948-19a1-4644-9c22-ee7c84719d49">Do one of the following:<ul><li data-uuid="3c7d0f43-6b07-45d0-a40f-f928409b1a02">Click the view in the Containment tree.</li><li data-uuid="220fab6f-5a55-4ee3-9d81-ad56b8d04635">Click the view tab.</li></ul></li></ul><h4>Zooming in or out of the active view</h4><p>To zoom in or out of the active view</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="66cde2a1-d593-4f63-a420-bb08b9176757">In the Zoom tab, drag the corners of the blue square.</li></ul><h4>Quickly accessing any part of the view</h4><p>To quickly access any part of the view</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="47f27ae9-f94a-4cbc-9e1d-c5a67592a11b">In the Zoom tab, drag the blue square to the desired part of the view. The selected view part will be displayed in the view pane.</li></ul><h4>Fitting the view to the window</h4><p>To fit the view to the window</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="8c24fcec-4bbc-4800-9d61-b93e782f9c9b">In the Zoom tab, on the blue square <ac:image ac:height="28" ac:width="26"><ri:attachment ri:filename="fit_window_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Quick Properties Panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, click the <strong>Fit in Window</strong> button or press <strong>CTRL+W</strong>.</li></ul><h3>Change Sets tab</h3><p>Use the Change Sets tab to track project modifications. The latest changes are compared against the last saved state (via <strong>Save Project</strong> or <strong>Ctrl+S </strong>commands) of the project in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><ul><li data-uuid="be74c481-063f-421a-bcb5-2319b365827b">The panel legend explains the modification markings.</li><li data-uuid="b99b5d43-e37c-4f17-9df4-3e813432ef51">Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> icon to update the display.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="change sets tab.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Quick Properties Panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005855 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00168: (2026x Refresh1) Redefinition

- page_id: `304005855`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005855/2026x+Refresh1+Redefinition
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:48.292+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Relations > (2026x Refresh1) Specializations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The redefinition relationshipis a kind of subsetting used to replace an inherited feature by redefining.

##### Redefinition

###### Redefining a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view

To redefine a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view

- Right-click an inherited usage element symbol in a view and in the shortcut menu select**Refactor**, then**Redefine**.

###### Redefining a usage via the Textual Editor

To redefine a usage via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the usage element that you want to be the redefining one.
2. Follow the element name with either one of the following:
  1. keyword redefines
  2. textual symbol :>>
3. Follow the keyword with the name of the redefined usage element.
4. Click the Synchronize button. partdefEngine{ 
partcylinders[4..8] :Cylinders; 
}partdieselEngine:Engine{ 
partdieselEngineCylinders[4] :>>cylinders; 
/* or */ 
partdieselEngineCylinders[4] redefinescylinders; 
}

###### Redefining a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To redefine a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] on an element symbol executing the Set Redefined Feature command.

###### Redefining a usage via the view palette

To redefine a usage via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, select redefinition .
2. Draw the line with the arrowhead going from the redefining element to the inherited one. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Redefining a usage via the Create Relation command

To redefine a usagevia the Create Relation command

1. Right-click the element that you want to be the redefining one.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click redefinition . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to redefine. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to redefine and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to redefine from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

###### Redefining a usage via the Specification panel

To redefine a usage via the Specification panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''] page.

##### Redefining parameters

Parameters are directional features used in behavioral contexts. When it comes to redefining parameters, each owned parameter implicitly redefines the corresponding inherited parameter according to the latter's order:

50%

actiondefImportCustomerData{ 
incustomerId; 
incustomerName; 
incustomerEmail; 
} 
actionimportCustomerData:ImportCustomerData{ 
incustomerId;// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerId 
incustomerName;// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerName 
incustomerEmail;// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerEmail 
incustomerPhoneNumber;// new owned parameter 
}

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you redefine the inherited parameters via either of the following procedures:

- 
- 

The redefinitions are created as follows:

- If there are other inherited parameters from the same owner with preceding positions, then all of those parameters are redefined. E.g., if there are three inherited parameters and you redefine the second, then the first and the second parameters are redefined, while the third is unaffected. [ATTACHMENT filename='parameter redefinition 2.png']
- The declared name for each redefining parameter is derived from the redefined parameter name. If multiple parameters are redefined, the names are concatenated. E.g., parameters customerId and validationId are inherited via separate specializations from two different definitions. Both parameters represent the first position in the order of inherited parameters. Redefining either of them creates a single redefining parameter with a concatenated name. [ATTACHMENT filename='parameter redefinition 3.png']
- The direction of each created parameter is the same as, or compatible with, the direction of the redefined parameter.

panel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefining a usage via the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p>The redefinitions are created as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="8b42643b-6ce5-4470-a66b-4f2dc1d02aa8">If there are other inherited parameters from the same owner with preceding positions, then all of those parameters are redefined. E.g., if there are three inherited parameters and you redefine the second, then the first and the second parameters are redefined, while the third is unaffected. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter redefinition 2.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="551561c5-c124-4d9e-a615-d050e39daae3">The declared name for each redefining parameter is derived from the redefined parameter name. If multiple parameters are redefined, the names are concatenated. E.g., parameters <em>customerId </em>and <em>validationId </em>are inherited via separate specializations from two different definitions. Both parameters represent the first position in the order of inherited parameters. Redefining either of them creates a single redefining parameter with a concatenated name.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter redefinition 3.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="4f92a41c-4772-4b83-85cb-23b1eb0c60dd">The direction of each created parameter is the same as, or compatible with, the direction of the redefined parameter.</li></ul>

## PAGE 00169: (2026x Refresh1) Reference subsetting

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:48.645+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Relations > (2026x Refresh1) Specializations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A reference subsettingis a kind of subsetting relationship. While a subsetting usage can have multiple subsetted usages, a reference subsetting usage can only have one specific subsetted usage.

##### Reference subsetting

###### Reference subsetting a usage via the Textual Editor

To reference subset a usage via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the usage element that you want to be the referencing one.
2. Follow the element name with either one of the following:
  1. keyword references
  2. textual symbol ::>
3. Follow the keyword with the name of the referenced usage element.
4. Click the Synchronize button. part'drone in delivery mission'{ 
perform action ::>'deliver goods'; 
/* or */ perform action references'deliver goods'; 
} 
action'deliver goods'{ 
action'start mission';action'fly to destination'; 
first'start mission'then'fly to destination'; 
}

###### Reference subsetting a usage via the view palette

To reference subset a usage via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, select reference subsetting .
2. Draw the line with the arrowhead going from the referencing usage to the referenced one. [ATTACHMENT filename='Reference subsetting.png']

###### Reference subsetting a usage via the Create Relation command

To reference subset a usagevia the Create Relation command

1. Right-click the element that you want to be the subsetting one.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click reference subsetting . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to subset. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to subset and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to subset from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

## PAGE 00171: (2026x Refresh1) Removing used projects

- page_id: `312246799`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/312246799/2026x+Refresh1+Removing+used+projects
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-06-10T10:45:39.635+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Project management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Managing local used projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Used projects are removed from the main project with all their references, including their used projects (indirectly used projects).

###### Removing a used project via the Containment tree

To remove a used project via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree of the main project, expand the Used Projects node.
2. Right-click a used project and select Remove .
3. When the question dialog opens, click Yes to confirm that you want to remove the used project. [ATTACHMENT filename='Removing a used project via the Containment tree.png']

###### Removing a used project via the Project Usages dialog

To remove a used project via the Project Usages dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing local used projects' space='']Open the **Project Usages**dialog and d o one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the **Remove with References** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. In the dialog's project usages tree, right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Remove with References**command.
2. When the question dialog opens, click Yes to confirm that you want to remove the used project.
3. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

###### Removing a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To remove a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing local used projects' space='']Open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog .
2. In the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog, do one of the following:
  1. S elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the **Remove with References** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. Right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Remove with References** command . The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states ' Remove, delete references '. [ATTACHMENT filename='Removing a used project via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog.png']
3. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the Reset Changes for Selection command.
4. Do one of the following:
  1. To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the Apply button.
  2. To save changes and close the dialog, click the OK button.
  3. To discard changes and close the dialog, click the Cancel button.
5. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006415 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00172: (2026x Refresh1) Rendering basics

- page_id: `304006415`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006415/2026x+Refresh1+Rendering+basics
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:28.597+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Rendering views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the basic procedures for working with renderings and rendering definitions.

##### Creating rendering usage and definition elements

###### Creating rendering usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create rendering usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *rendering def*for a rendering definition.
  2. *rendering* for a rendering usage.
2. Specify the element name.
3. (Optional) Specify the rendering's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

renderingdefGraphicalRendering;

renderingasTreeDiagram : GraphicalRendering;

###### Creating concern usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create concern usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. rendering def to create a rendering definition element.
  2. rendering to create a rendering usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the rendering.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005118 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00173: (2026x Refresh1) Rendering views

- page_id: `304005118`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005118/2026x+Refresh1+Rendering+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:09.284+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A rendering feature specifies how a particular view and its exposed elements are supposed to be rendered, i.e., displayed for viewing. Once you have[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating views' space=''], specified the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Exposing elements for views' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Filtering elements for views' space=''] the exposed elements, you can specify the renderer for the view.

##### Renderings, rendering styles, and view definitions

The following table maps renderings and rendering styles available in the modeling tool to corresponding predefined view definitions.

| View rendering kind | Rendering style | Renderings | Subsets of the renderings | Rendering definitions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Graphical | Nested-view | Views::asInterconnectionDiagramView definitions using the renderer:Standard predefined symbolic view definitions:'General View' (through inheritance from 'Symbolic View' view def) 'Interconnection View' (through inheritance from 'Symbolic View' view def) 'Action Flow View' (through inheritance from 'Symbolic View' view def)'State Transition View' (through inheritance from 'Symbolic View' view def)Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions:NestedViewUsagesNestedView (by subclassifying NestedView)PartsNestedView (by subclassifying UsagesNestedView)Parts&PortsNestedView (by subclassifying UsagesNestedView)ActionsNestedView (by subclassifying UsagesNestedView)StatesNestedView (by subclassifying UsagesNestedView) | - | Views::GraphicalRendering |
| Tree-view | Views::asTreeDiagramView definitions using the renderer:Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions:TreeViewPartsTreeView (by subclassifying TreeView)ActionsTreeView (by subclassifying TreeView)RequirementTreeView (by subclassifying TreeView) | - | Views::GraphicalRendering |  |
| Tabular | - | Views::asElementTable | DS_Views::CoreViews::asTableView definitions using the renderer:Standard predefined tabular view definitions:'Generic Table' (subsets asTable inherited from 'Base Table' via subclassification)'Requirements Table' (subsets asTable inherited from 'Base Table' via subclassification) | Views::TabularRendering |

##### Rendering views

Ways to render views:

- . You can render views through inheritance, e.g., if the view is defined by a view definition that contains a renderer, the renderer is inherited by and applied to the defined view.
- . You can specify a renderer after the expose and filter declarations directly for the specific view, using the keyword render .

###### Inheriting a renderer

Generally, renderings are specified for view definitions, which are then used to define specific view usages, the latter inheriting the renderer specified for its definition.

See the table for all renderings available in the modeling tool and the predefined view definitions that use them. Choose the view definition based on the renderer specified for it that would be the most suitable for your view, then define the view by the view definition. However, many predefined view definitions carry specific filters and other customizations that will be inherited in addition to the renderer, thus choose accordingly. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbolic views' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Tabular views' space=''] pages.

The corresponding procedures are:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating symbolic views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating symbolic views' space=''] page.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating symbolic views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating symbolic views' space=''] page.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating tabular views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating tabular views' space=''] page.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating tabular views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating tabular views' space=''] page.

#### PANEL: Explanation in textual notation

Explanation in textual notation

// explanation and example of a symbolic view *General View*inheriting a renderer through subclassification

abstractviewdef<sv>'Symbolic View':>Visualization{ 
//... 
renderViews::asInterconnectionDiagram; //view definition has the *Views::asInterconnectionDiagram***renderer specified for it 
}

abstractviewdef<bsv>'Base Symbolic View':>sv{ // view definition 'Base Symbolic View' inherits the *Views::asInterconnectionDiagram***renderer through subclassification of 'Symbolic View' 
partbaseViewPalette:ViewPalettes::BasePalette:>>palette; 
}

viewdef<gv>'General View':>bsv,StandardViewDefinitions::gv{// view definition 'General View' inherits the*Views::asInterconnectionDiagram***renderer through subclassification of 'Base Symbolic View' 
partgeneralViewPalette:GeneralPalette:>>baseViewPalette; // view definition also contains a specific view palette 
}

view'General View':DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv; // the specific view is defined by the view definition 'General View', inheriting its renderer and view palette

// explanation and example of a tabular view *Generic Table* inheriting a renderer through subclassification

abstractviewdef<bt>'Base Table':>bgv{ // view definition *'Base Table'* has the *asTable*renderer specified for it 
renderasTable; 
}

viewdef<gt>'Generic Table':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bt{ // view definition 'Generic Table' subsets the *asTable*renderer inherited through subclassification of *'Base Table'* 
renderrenderingasTable:>>asTable; 
}

view'Generic Table':DS_Views::TabularViews::gt;// the specific view is defined by the view definition 'Generic Table', inheriting its renderer

###### Specifying a renderer

Instead of creating views defined by view definitions that already have specified renderers, you can specify therenderer directly for the new view.

The corresponding procedures are:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating symbolic views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating symbolic views' space=''] page.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating tabular views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating tabular views' space=''] page.

#### PANEL: Explanation in textual notation

Explanation in textual notation

viewnewView{ // new view element is not defined by any view definition 
renderViews::asInterconnectionDiagram; // the view has a renderer *asInterconnectionDiagram*, rendering it as a 'General View' symbolic view with nested-view rendering style 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A rendering feature specifies how a particular view and its exposed elements are supposed to be rendered, i.e., displayed for viewing. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Once you have </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[created a view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">, specified the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exposing elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[exposed elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filtered]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the exposed elements, you can specify the renderer for the view. </span></p><h3 style="text-align: left;">Renderings, rendering styles, and view definitions</h3><p style="text-align: left;">The following table maps renderings and rendering styles available in the modeling tool to corresponding predefined view definitions. </p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 78.0816%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 15.3863%;" /><col style="width: 14.0522%;" /><col style="width: 36.02%;" /><col style="width: 41.6231%;" /><col style="width: 21.0784%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: center;">View rendering kind</th><th style="text-align: center;">Rendering style</th><th style="text-align: center;">Renderings</th><th style="text-align: center;">Subsets of the renderings</th><th style="text-align: center;">Rendering definitions</th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">Graphical</td><td>Nested-view</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><em>Views::asInterconnectionDiagram</em></p><p><strong>View definitions using the renderer:</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="f8f18623-ede6-42f3-b32b-161c014ace7a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Standard predefined symbolic view definitions:<br /></span><ul><li><p>'<em>General View'</em> (through inheritance from '<em>Symbolic View</em>' view def) </p></li><li><p><em>'Interconnection View</em>' (through inheritance from '<em>Symbolic View</em>' view def) </p></li><li><p><em>'Action Flow View' </em>(through inheritance from '<em>Symbolic View</em>' view def)</p></li><li><p>'<em>State Transition View'</em> (through inheritance from '<em>Symbolic View</em>' view def)</p></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="c290f84c-1d77-4804-91bc-1a477b0c854e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions:</span><ul><li><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">NestedView</span></em></li><li><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">UsagesNestedView<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span></span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(by subclassifying</span></span><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> NestedView</span></span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">)</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em>PartsNestedView </em>(by subclassifying <em>UsagesNestedView</em>)</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Parts&amp;<em>PortsNestedView </em>(by subclassifying <em>Usages</em><em>NestedView</em>)</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em>ActionsNestedView </em>(by subclassifying <em>Usages</em><em>NestedView</em>)</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em>StatesNestedView </em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(by subclassifying <em>Usages</em><em>NestedView</em>)</span></span></li></ul></li></ul></div></td><td><p>-</p></td><td><p><em>Views::GraphicalRendering </em></p></td></tr><tr><td>Tree-view</td><td><p><em>Views::asTreeDiagram</em></p><p><strong>View definitions using the renderer:</strong></p><ul><li>Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions:<ul><li><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">TreeView</span></em></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em>PartsTreeView </em>(by subclassifying <em>TreeView</em>)</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em>ActionsTreeView </em>(by subclassifying <em>TreeView</em>)</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em>RequirementTreeView </em>(by subclassifying <em>TreeView</em>)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td><td><p>-</p></td><td><p><em>Views::GraphicalRendering </em></p></td></tr><tr><td>Tabular</td><td>-</td><td><em>Views::asElementTable</em></td><td><p><em>DS_Views::CoreViews::asTable</em></p><p><strong>View definitions using the renderer:<br /></strong></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Standard predefined tabular view definitions:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">'<em>Generic Table</em>' (subsets <em>asTable</em> inherited from '<em>Base Table</em>' via subclassification)</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">'<em>Requirements Table</em>' (subsets <em>asTable</em> inherited from '<em>Base Table</em>' via subclassification)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td><td><p><em>Views::TabularRendering</em></p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Rendering views</h3><p>Ways to render views:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Inheriting a renderer" />. You can render views through inheritance, e.g., if the view is defined by a view definition that contains a renderer, the renderer is inherited by and applied to the defined view. </li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying a renderer" />. You can specify a renderer after the <em>expose </em>and <em>filter</em> declarations directly for the specific view, using the keyword <em>render</em><span>.</span></li></ul><h4>Inheriting a renderer</h4><p>Generally, renderings are specified for view definitions, which are then used to define specific view usages, the latter inheriting the renderer specified for its definition.</p><p>See the <ac:link ac:anchor="Renderings, rendering styles, and view definitions" /> table for all renderings available in the modeling tool and the predefined view definitions that use them. Choose the view definition based on the renderer specified for it that would be the most suitable for your view, then define the view by the view definition. However, many predefined view definitions carry specific filters and other customizations that will be inherited in addition to the renderer, thus choose accordingly. For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Symbolic views" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Tabular views" /></ac:link> pages.</p><p>The corresponding procedures are:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating symbolic views via the Create View command"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating symbolic views via the Create View command]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating symbolic views" /></ac:link> page.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating symbolic views via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating symbolic views via the Textual Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating symbolic views" /></ac:link> page.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating tabular views via the Create View command"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating tabular views via the Create View command]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating tabular views" /></ac:link> page.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating tabular views via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating tabular views" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005696 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00174: (2026x Refresh1) Rendering views basics

- page_id: `304005696`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005696/2026x+Refresh1+Rendering+views+basics
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:44.029+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Rendering views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two ways a rendering usage can be specified for a view:

50%

- by referencing the rendering usage defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the render keyword (unnamed element) or the rendering keyword (named element) . [ATTACHMENT filename='rendering.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

50%

- by containing an owned rendering usage, i.e., the view renderer is the owned rendering usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the rendering keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='rendering2.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 

##### Rendering views by referencing renderings defined elsewhere in the model

###### Rendering views via the Textual Editor

To render views via the Textual Editor

1. In the body of a view for which you want to reference a rendering, declare one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed rendering, declare the render keyword, then follow with the name of the rendering you want to reference.
  2. For a named rendering, declare the rendering keyword, name the new usage, and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space=''] the rendering you want to reference.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

viewvehicleModel{ 
renderasTreeDiagram;// unnamed rendering 
renderingasNewTreeDiagram::>asTreeDiagram;// named rendering 
}

###### Rendering views via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To render views via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop a rendering element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on a rendering usage element symbol (keyword render rendering ) that you want to render the view by referencing the rendering using the Create Satisfy Requirement command.

###### Rendering views via the Specification panel

To render views via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop a rendering element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on the **Referenced Rendering** field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the rendering usage element (keyword render rendering ). The appropriate rendering is specified for the element.

##### Rendering views via the owned rendering usage itself as the renderer

###### Rendering views via the Textual Editor2Rendering views via the Textual Editor

To render views via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the view's body, declare the rendering keyword, specify the element name, and then the rendering's body.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

viewvehicleModel{renderingasCustomTreeDiagram:GraphicalRendering;// // new view-owned rendering usage}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005829 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00175: (2026x Refresh1) Require constraint

- page_id: `304005829`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005829/2026x+Refresh1+Require+constraint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:47.306+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements > (2026x Refresh1) Requirement constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A require constraint states the required constraints imposed on the model, expressed formally or informally in a requirement or a use case.

There are two ways these constraints can be required:

50%

- by referencing a constraint defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the require keyword (unnamed element) or the require constraint keyword (named element). 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 

50%

- by containing an owned require constraint usage, i.e., the required constraint is the require constraint usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the require constraint keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='require constraint 1.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

##### Requiring a constraint by referencing a constraint defined elsewhere in the model

###### Requiring constraints via the Textual Editor

To require constraints via the Textual Editor

1. In the body of an element for which you want to reference a constraint, declare one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed require constraint, declare the require keyword, then follow with the name of the constraint you want to reference.
  2. For a named require constraint, declare the require constraint keyword, name the new usage, and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Reference subsetting' space=''] the constraint you want to reference.
2. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.) in the element's body.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

constraintmassLimit{ 
attributemass:MassValue; 
attributemassLimit:MassValue; 
mass<=massLimit 
} 
requirementdefMaximumMass{ 
attributemassActual:MassValue; 
attributemassRequired:MassValue;

requiremassLimit{// step 1.a - unnamed usage reference subsets another constraint usage 
:>>mass=massActual; 
:>>massLimit=massRequired; 
}

requireconstraintmLimit::>massLimit{// step 1.b - named usage reference subsets another constraint usage 
:>>mass=massActual; 
:>>massLimit=massRequired; 
} 
}

###### Requiring constraints via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To require constraints via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop a constraint element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on:
  - the requirement element symbol that you want to require the constraint by referencing it using the **Create Require Constraint** command.
  - the use case element symbol that you want to require the constraint by referencing it using the **Create Objective**or **Create Require Constraint of Objective** commands.
  - the require constraint symbol that you want to require by referencing the constraint using the **Set Required Constraint****** command.

###### Requiring constraints via the Specification panel

To require constraints via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop a constraint element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on the **Required Constraint** field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the require constraint element. The appropriate constraint is specified for the element.

###### Requiring constraints via the view palette

To require constraints via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Requirements group, click require .
2. Do one of the following:
  1. To require an existing constraint, in the view pane, click on the use case or requirement element symbol, then on the constraint element symbol.
  2. To require a new constraint, in the view pane, click on the use case or requirement element symbol, then elsewhere in the view pane to create a requirement element symbol. The require path is drawn between the element symbols.

###### Requiring constraints via the smart manipulator in a view

To require constraints via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view , do one of the following:
  - To require an existing constraint, select the use case element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the require command. Click on the requirement or constraint element symbol.
  - To create a new require constraint, select the use case element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the require command. Click elsewhere in the view pane to create a requirement element symbol . The require path is drawn between the element symbols.

##### Requiring a constraint via the owned require constraint usage itself as the required constraint

###### Requiring constraints via the Textual Editor1Requiring constraints via the Textual Editor

To require constraints via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword require constraint for a require constraint usage
2. Specify the element name.
3. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.) in the element's body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

requirementdefMaximumMass{ 
attributemassActual:MassValue; 
attributemassRequired:MassValue;

requireconstraintmLimit{// named constraint 
massActual<=massRequired 
} 
}

###### Requiring constraints via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''] page.

To require constraints via the symbol compartment in a view

1. Do one of the following:
  1. Select an element symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the require constraint element.
  2. Select an element symbol and on the right side of the require constraints compartment, click the Create require constraint button.
2. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.). The new element is created and displayed in the require constraints compartment.

###### Requiring constraints via the Specification panel1Requiring constraints via the Specification panel

To require constraints via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **require constraint**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **require constraints**compartment section's bar, click the **Create require constraint**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
3. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.). The element is created in the require constraints compartment.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">massActual</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">massLimit</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> }</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">require</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span
