}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Requiring constraints via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p>To require constraints via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="b08490db-c355-466f-b93e-0bb97435a800">Drag and drop a constraint element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler" /></ac:link> and drop it on:<ul><li data-uuid="56d3c6c3-d5a4-4549-89fe-6d68288815ab"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">the requirement element symbol that you want to require the constraint </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">by referencing it </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">using the</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <strong>Create Require Constraint</strong> </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">command.</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span></li><li data-uuid="dea48ba8-07d3-44e7-a394-5d229ce47d4c"><span>the use case element symbol that you want to require the constraint </span><span>by referencing it </span><span>using the</span><span> <strong>Create Objective </strong>or <strong>Create Require Constraint of Objective</strong> </span><span>commands.</span><span> </span></li><li data-uuid="67d56d33-1c75-4655-8fb3-01bea1412479"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">the require constraint symbol that you want to require </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">by referencing the constraint </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">using the</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <strong>Set Required Constraint</strong><strong> </strong></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">command.</span></li></ul></li></ul><h4>Requiring constraints via the Specification panel</h4><p>To require constraints via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="f411169f-057f-458a-bd99-483981e9cbfa">Drag and drop a constraint element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler" /></ac:link> and drop it on the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Required Constraint</strong></span><strong> </strong>field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the require constraint element. <span><br /></span>The appropriate constraint is specified for the element.</li></ul><h4>Requiring constraints via the view palette</h4><p><span>To require constraints via the view palette</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c6bc69bd-3d4e-4319-adcf-4d288ef9d5e3">In the view palette, under the Requirements group, click <strong>require</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="bf6078d2-e7cb-47bf-bc25-a99d6d8e4c11">Do one of the following:<ol><li><span>To require an existing constraint, in the view pane, click on the use case or requirement element symbol, then on the constraint element symbol. </span></li><li><span>To require a new constraint, in the view pane, click on the use case or requirement element symbol, then elsewhere in the view pane to create a requirement element symbol. <br /></span>The <strong>require</strong><span> path is drawn between the element symbols.</span></li></ol></li></ol><h4><span>Requiring constraints via the smart manipulator in a view</span></h4><p><span>To require constraints via the smart manipulator in a view</span></p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="929348d7-4240-4183-97da-9fbaf254e646">In a<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">view</span>, do one of the following:<ul><li><span>To require an existing constraint, </span>select the use case element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the<strong><span> require </span></strong>command. Click on the requirement or constraint element symbol.</li><li><span>To create a new require constraint, </span>select the use case element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the<strong><span> require </span></strong>command. Click <span>elsewhere in the view pane to create a requirement element symbol</span>.<br />The <strong>require</strong><span> path is drawn between the element symbols.</span></li></ul></li></ul><h3>Requiring a constraint via the owned require constraint usage itself as the required constraint</h3><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bdde8ebe-b12c-4ebf-9b0f-c906eb937ad9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Requiring constraints via the Textual Editor1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Requiring constraints via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To require constraints via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1beda556-18ed-4414-886c-24443c36324f">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword <em>require constraint</em><span> for a require constraint usage</span></li><li data-uuid="f34a4115-1158-45ac-9103-818b8381dad8"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span>Specify the element name</span>.</span></li><li data-uuid="51622af2-024d-4d3f-8d9d-4ef17a959acc">Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.) in the element's body.</li><li data-uuid="f0418ed4-cf23-4853-9de3-1b85266bb0cc">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45267177-5718-40d9-a930-045919892f8a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MaximumMass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">massActual</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MassValue</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">massRequired</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MassValue</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">require</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">constraint</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mLimit</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {                   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named constraint</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">massActual</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> &lt;= </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">massRequired</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Requiring constraints via the symbol compartment in a view</h4><p>For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Compartments" /></ac:link> page.</p><p>To require constraints via the symbol compartment in a view</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="4bfb1473-f664-497d-a864-9c8184038a72">Do one of the following:<ol><li>Select an element symbol and click the<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span> </span>button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the <strong>require constraint </strong>element.</li><li>Select an element symbol and on the right side of the <strong>require constraints </strong>compartment, click the<span> </span><strong>Create require constraint </strong>button.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="3af05cfd-8e4f-4684-ac3a-4083fc828e39">Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.).<br />The new element is created and displayed in the <strong>require constraints </strong><span>compartment.</span></li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6205a71-0439-496c-8ecc-3e57da454333"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Requiring constraints via the Specification panel1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Requiring constraints via the Specification panel</h4><p>To require constraints via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="9dd4ae4e-0366-401d-9218-47e2b7988fef"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="88043472-88be-4823-a4bb-400839021ddb"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>require constraint </strong>element in the shortcut menu.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>require constraints </strong>compartment section's bar, click the <strong>Create require constraint </strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="d1d82133-8b83-4135-b326-43a3e8a54e4e">Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.).<br />The element is created in the <strong>require constraints </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">compartment.</span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006418 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00176: (2026x Refresh1) Requirement and constraint evaluation

- page_id: `304006418`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006418/2026x+Refresh1+Requirement+and+constraint+evaluation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:28.734+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using views is the quickest way to evaluate multiple requirements and constraints at once. When the evaluation is complete, the view shapes are highlighted with icons or colors to indicate if requirements and constraints are satisfied or not. Failing and passing values are highlighted as well.You can also use the Containment tree,**SysML v2 Model Evaluation**console, or a view toevaluate individual satisfy requirements and assert constraints.

##### Evaluating requirements and constraints in views

To evaluate requirements and constraints in views

1. Open the view displaying the elements that have related requirements and constraints you want to evaluate.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='evaluate_button.png'] in the view toolbar. To evaluate a single element or it's attribute displayed in a view, select the element shape or an attribute in the shape compartment before completing this step.To evaluate an individual satisfy requirement or assert constraint, select the element shape before completing this step. The evaluation result is *true* or *false*.

The view shapes are highlighted indicating whether related system requirements and constraints pass or fail.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A view with requirement/constraint evaluation results.

###### Evaluation results in views

The following table explains the requirement and constraint evaluation results displayed on view shapes

| Annotation | Description | Tooltip |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ORA shape or its header is colored in green | The annotated elements satisfy related system requirements or constraints. | N/A |
| ORA shape or its header is colored in yellow | The annotated elements indirectly fail related system requirements or constraints. This means that system requirements or constraints are failed by inner parts of the annotated element. | Hover over the icon to see which inner elements fail system requirements or constraints. |
| ORA shape or its header is colored in red | The annotated elements fail related system requirements or constraints. | Hover over the icon to see which requirement or constraint failed. |
|  | The annotated values satisfy related system requirements or constraints. | N/A |
|  | The annotated values fail related system requirements or constraints. | Hover over the value to see the text of the failing requirement. |

###### Clearing evaluation results in views

To clear evaluation results in a view

- In the view toolbar, click an arrow next to [ATTACHMENT filename='evaluate_button.png'] and select Clear Values .

###### Customizing visual representation of evaluation results

You can customize how requirement and constraint verification results are displayed in views.

To customize visual representation of evaluation results in a view

1. In the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, select the Evaluation option group.
3. Specify the following options:
  - Display Verification Results As - Select Display Icons to add icons to shape headers. Select Paint Headers to color shapes or their headers.
  - Propagate Deep Nested Failures - Set to true to indicate nested requirement and constraint failures. Set to false to only highlight the shapes of the elements that directly fail a requirement or constraint.
4. Click OK to close the Project Options dialog.

##### Evaluating requirements and constraints via the Containment tree

To evaluate a requirement or constraint via the Containment tree

- In the Containment tree, right-click a satisfy requirement or an assert constraint and select Evaluate . [ATTACHMENT filename='evaluating_requirement_via_containment_tree.png']

The evaluation result (*true* or *false*) is displayed in the**SysML v2 Model Evaluation**console.

##### Evaluating requirements and constraints via the SysML v2 Model Evaluation console

To evaluate a requirement or constraint via the **SysML v2 Model Evaluation** console

1. Select Window > SysML v2 Model Evaluation to open the SysML v2 Model Evaluation console.
2. In the input box of the console, type the qualified name of the satisfy requirement or assert constraint you want to evaluate and press Enter. To save time, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the toolbar of the **SysML v2 Model Evaluation** console to enable context selection in the Containment tree. When context selection is enabled, select the owner the element you want to evaluate and enter only the element's name to evaluate it. [ATTACHMENT filename='evaluating_requirement_via_console.png']

The evaluation result (*true*or*false*) is displayed in the**SysML v2 Model Evaluation**console.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Failing and passing values are highlighted as well. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You can also use <span>the Containment tree, </span><strong>SysML v2 Model Evaluation</strong><span> console, or a view to </span>evaluate individual satisfy requirements and assert constraints.</span></p><h3>Evaluating requirements and constraints in views</h3><p>To evaluate requirements and constraints in views</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the view displaying the elements that have related requirements and constraints you want to evaluate.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="evaluate_button.png" /></ac:image> in the view toolbar.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e1d82617-d089-49d6-ab13-b70cf6ad59f1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>To evaluate a single element or it's attribute displayed in a view, select the element shape or an attribute in the shape compartment before completing this step.</li><li>To evaluate an individual satisfy requirement or assert constraint, select the element shape before completing this step. <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Options</strong>  dialog, select the <strong>Evaluation</strong> option group.</li><li>Specify the following options:<ul><li><strong>Display Verification Results As</strong> - Select <strong>Display Icons</strong> to add icons to shape headers. Select <strong>Paint Headers</strong> to color shapes or their headers.</li><li><strong>Propagate Deep Nested Failures</strong> - Set to <em>true</em> to indicate nested requirement and constraint failures. Set to <em>false</em> to only highlight the shapes of the elements that directly fail a requirement or constraint.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog.</li></ol><h3>Evaluating requirements and constraints via the Containment tree</h3><p>To evaluate a requirement or constraint via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Containment tree, right-click a satisfy requirement or an assert constraint and select <strong>Evaluate</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="evaluating_requirement_via_containment_tree.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The evaluation result (<em>true</em> or <em>false</em>) is displayed in the </span><strong>SysML v2 Model Evaluation</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>console.</span></p><h3><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Evaluating requirements and constraints via the SysML v2 Model Evaluation console</span></h3><p>To evaluate a requirement or constraint via the <strong>SysML v2 Model Evaluation</strong> console</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Window</strong> &gt; <strong>SysML v2 Model Evaluation</strong> to open the <strong>SysML v2 Model Evaluation</strong> console.</li><li>In the input box of the console, type the qualified name of the satisfy requirement or assert constraint you want to evaluate and press Enter.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7ef7365-c601-472f-a96c-786fb881295e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To save time, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="context_selection_icon.png" /></ac:image> in the toolbar of the <strong>SysML v2 Model Evaluation</strong> console to enable context selection in the Containment tree. ## PAGE 00177: (2026x Refresh1) Requirement constraints

- page_id: `304004681`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:17:41.993+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Constraints allow you to specify the requirement eitherinformally using textstatements (commonly known as "shall" statements) or more formally using constraint expressions.

A requirement constraint is built from two sets of other constraints: the assumed and required constraints of the requirement.

- A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Require constraint' space='']**require**constraint states the required constraints imposed on the model, expressed formally or informally in the requirement.
- An [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Assume constraint' space='']**assume**constraint states the assumed constraints imposed on the model, expressed formally or informally in the requirement.

Required and assumed constraints are declared as composite constraint usages in the body of a requirement definition or usage. The required constraints of a requirement only apply if all the assumed constraints are satisfied. A requirement is satisfied for a subject only when both constraints are true.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>Constraints allow you to specify the requirement either </span><span>informally using text </span>statements (commonly known as &quot;shall&quot; statements) or more formally using constraint expressions.</p><p>A requirement constraint is built from two sets of other constraints: the assumed and required constraints of the requirement. </p><ul><li data-uuid="19943b9a-3643-4bdf-98e3-6249b147b0d9">A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Require constraint" /><ac:link-body><strong>require </strong>constraint</ac:link-body></ac:link> states the required constraints imposed on the model, expressed formally or informally in the requirement.</li><li data-uuid="fada4ed3-7740-427e-a542-7a6af92f184c">An <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Assume constraint" /><ac:link-body><strong>assume </strong>constraint</ac:link-body></ac:link> states the assumed constraints imposed on the model, expressed formally or informally in the requirement.</li></ul><p>Required and assumed constraints are declared as composite constraint usages in the body of a requirement definition or usage. The required constraints of a requirement only apply if all the assumed constraints are satisfied. A requirement is satisfied for a subject only when both constraints are true.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004686 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00178: (2026x Refresh1) Requirement decomposition

- page_id: `304004686`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004686/2026x+Refresh1+Requirement+decomposition
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:17:42.338+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A requirement definition or usage may be decomposed into a requirements tree of nested requirement usages (subrequirements), which may themselves be further decomposed. Any nested requirement usage is automatically considered to be a required constraint of the containing requirement definition or usage, i.e., nested requirements must be satisfied for the containing requirement to be satisfied. Requirements can be grouped either by composition, or reference.

##### Decomposing requirement definitions

You can represent the next level of decomposition of a requirement definition using the feature typing (*defined by*) relationship.

###### Decomposing requirement definitions via the Textual Editor

To decompose requirement definitions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the requirement usage that you want to define, nested within a requirement definition.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Feature typing' space=''] . requirementdefVehicleSpecification{ 
requirementfullVehicleMassLimit:MassRequirement;// requirement usage, owned by a requirement definition, defined by another requirement definition 
requirementemptyVehicleMassLimit:MassRequirement; 
}requirementdefMassRequirement;

###### Displaying decompositions of requirement definitions in a view

To display decompositions of requirement definitions in a view

- In a view, select the requirement definition symbol and the requirement usage symbol it defines, then use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command. The feature typing relationship is displayed.

##### Decomposing composite requirement usages

You can display a requirements tree, i.e., how a requirement is composed of other requirements, which can be done by one of the following options:

- composition - defines owned subrequirements via the feature membership relationship. In the textual notation, subrequirements are nested in the body between the curly braces of the owning requirement.
- reference - defines requirements not owned by the overall requirement, which only references other requirements as subrequirements. In this case, subrequirements are modeled as required constraints in graphical and textual notations.

###### Decomposing composite requirement usages via the Textual Editor

To decompose composite requirement usages via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the requirement you want to decompose, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating requirements' space=''] .
2. Click the Synchronize button. requirement vehicleSpecification:VehicleSpecification{ 
 requirement fullVehicleMassLimit : MassRequirement; // the owning requirement usage with nested requirement usages 
 requirement emptyVehicleMassLimit : MassRequirement; 
}

###### Decomposing composite requirement usages via a view by creating a composite requirement

To decompose composite requirement usages via a view by creating a composite requirement

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating requirements' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the requirement for which you want to create a composite requirement.
2. Create an owned requirement in one of the following ways:
  1. Via the view palette (requirement symbol option):
    1. In the view palette, under the Requirements group, click the requirement button to create a requirement usage element.
    2. Click on the requirement symbol displayed in the view pane to create a requirement owned by another.
  2. Via the view palette (feature membership option):
    1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the feature membership button.
    2. First, click on the requirement symbol displayed in the view pane and then click anywhere in the view pane to create a requirement owned by another.
  3. Via the smart manipulator (feature membership option):
    1. Click the requirement symbol for which you want to create a nested requirement.
    2. In its smart manipulator, click the feature membership icon.
    3. Click anywhere in the view pane to create a requirement owned by another.

###### Decomposing composite requirement usages via a view by designating an existing requirement as a composite one

To decompose composite requirement usages via a view by designating an existing requirement as a composite one

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating requirements' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the requirements you want to designate as the owner and the owned.
2. Designate an existing requirement as owned by another in one of the following ways:
  1. Via the view palette (feature membership option):
    1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the feature membership button.
    2. First, click on the requirement symbol you want to designate as the owner, then the one you want to designate as the owned one.
  2. Via the smart manipulator (feature membership option):
    1. Click the requirement symbol you want to designate as the owner.
    2. In its smart manipulator, click the feature membership icon, then click on the requirement you want to designate as the owned one.

###### Displaying decompositions of composite requirement usages in a view

To display decompositions of composite requirement usages in a view

- In a view, select the owning requirement usage symbol and use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command under the Tree-View Style category. The feature membership and the owned features (requirement usages) are displayed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Decomposing referencial requirement usages

In addition to having owned subrequirements, a requirement can also reference other requirements.

###### Decomposing referencial requirement usages via the Textual Editor

To decompose referencial requirement usages via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the curly braces of the requirement body within which you want to reference other existing requirements, declare the *require* keyword.
2. Follow with the name of the requirement you want to reference.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. requirementvehicleSpecification:VehicleSpecification{ // the grouping requirement 
doc/* Overall vehicle requirements group */ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
actorenvironment:Environment; 
requirefullVehicleMassLimit; // the requirement *fullVehicleMassLimit* is grouped by reference via the *require* keyword 
requireemptyVehicleMassLimit; // the requirement *emptyVehicleMassLimit* is grouped by referencevia the *require* keyword 
}// The subject of the following subrequirements are implicitly bound to the subject "vehicle" of the containing requirement.requirement fullVehicleMassLimit : MassRequirement { // the requirement that is grouped via the *require* keyword 
 attribute :>> massRequired = 2000 [kilogram]; 
} 
requirement emptyVehicleMassLimit : MassRequirement { // the requirement that is grouped via the *require* keyword; 
 attribute :>> massRequired = 1500 [kilogram]; 
}

###### Decomposing referencial requirement usages via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To decompose referencial requirement usages via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop a requirement element or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler on another requirement element symbol using the Create Require Constraint command. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] page.

###### Displaying decompositions of referencial requirement usages in a view

The required constraints are displayed in the requirement symbol's compartment 'require constraints'. However, you can also display them separately.

To display decompositions of referencial requirement usages in a view

- In a view, select the owning requirement usage symbol and use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command. The requirements, feature memberships, and required constraints are displayed. **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

##### Reusing and modifying the requirement tree

A requirement can subset another requirement facilitating requirement tree reuse and modification. The subsetting requirement inherits the requirement decomposition and other features. You can then modify the inherited requirements and features through redefinition and subsetting in addition to adding requirements.

###### Reusing and modifying a requirement tree via the Textual Editor

To reuse and modify a requirement tree via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating requirements' space=''] you want to reuse, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Subsetting' space=''] it.
2. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Redefinition' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Subsetting' space=''] the requirement features in the element body.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

requirementvehicleSpecification:VehicleSpecification{// subsetted (reused) requirement 
requirementfullVehicleMassLimit: MassRequirement; 
requirementemptyVehicleMassLimit: MassRequirement; 
}

requirementnewVehicleSpecification:>vehicleSpecification{// a requirement reuses the requirement tree by subsetting another requirement 
requirementfullNewVehicleMassLimit:>fullVehicleMassLimit; 
}

###### Reusing a requirement tree via the view palette

To reuse a requirement tree via the view palette

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating requirements' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the requirement whose decomposition you want to reuse.
2. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, click the subsetting button.
3. Click on the requirement you want to denote as the subsetting one and then click on the requirement you want to denote as the subsetted (reused) one.

###### Displaying the requirement tree reuse in a view

To display the requirement tree reuse in a view

1. In a view, select the subsetting and the subsetted requirement usage symbols, then u se the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command . The subsetting relationship is displayed.
2. (Optional) To display owned features of the subsetting or subsetted requirements, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command. The owned features (requirement usages) are displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='Displaying the requirement tree reuse on a diagram.png']

style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">massRequired</span> = <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2000</span> [<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">kilogram</span>];<br />}<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">emptyVehicleMassLimit</span> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MassRequirement</span> {    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the requirement that is grouped via the <em>require</em> keyword; </span><br />   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">massRequired</span> = <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1500</span> [<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">kilogram</span>];<br />}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Decomposing referencial requirement usages via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p>To decompose referencial requirement usages via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag and drop a requirement element or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler on another requirement element symbol using the<span> </span><strong>Create Require Constraint </strong>command.<span> </span><span>See the</span><span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler" /></ac:link><span> </span><span>page.</span></li></ul><h4>Displaying decompositions of referencial requirement usages in a view</h4><p>The required constraints are displayed in the requirement symbol's compartment 'require constraints'. However, you can also display them separately.</p><p>To display decompositions of referencial requirement usages in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>In a view, select the owning requirement usage symbol and use the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Features]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>command.<br />The requirements, feature memberships, and required constraints are displayed. <br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying decompositions of referencial requirement usages on a diagram 2.png" /></ac:image></strong></span></li></ul><h3>Reusing and modifying the requirement tree</h3><p>A requirement can subset another requirement facilitating requirement tree reuse and modification. The subsetting requirement inherits the requirement decomposition and other features. You can then modify the inherited requirements and features through redefinition and subsetting in addition to adding requirements.</p><h4>Reusing and modifying a requirement tree via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To reuse and modify a requirement tree via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[declare the requirement usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> you want to reuse, then <ac:link ac:anchor="Subsetting a usage via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subset]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> it.</li><li>(Optional) <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subset]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the requirement features in the element body. </li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8811e49e-ed80-4671-a9c5-db96a9276d25"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleSpecification </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">VehicleSpecification </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);">    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// subsetted (reused) requirement</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fullVehicleMassLimit</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : MassRequirement;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">emptyVehicleMassLimit</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : MassRequirement;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">newVehicleSpecification</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleSpecification</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a requirement reuses the requirement tree by subsetting another requirement</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fullNewVehicleMassLimit</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fullVehicleMassLimit</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);">  </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Reusing a requirement tree via the view palette</h4><p>To reuse a requirement tree via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[display]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the requirement whose decomposition you want to reuse.</li><li>In the view palette, under the Specializations group, click the <strong>subsetting </strong>button.</li><li>Click on the requirement you want to denote as the subsetting one and then click on the requirement you want to denote as the subsetted (reused) one.</li></ol><h4>Displaying the requirement tree reuse in a view</h4><p>To display the requirement tree reuse in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, select the subsetting and the subsetted requirement usage symbols, then u<span>se the </span><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display In-Between Relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong><span>command</span><span>. The subsetting relationship is displayed.</span></li><li>(Optional) To display owned features of the subsetting or subsetted requirements, use the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Features]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>command. The owned features (requirement usages) are displayed.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying the requirement tree reuse on a diagram.png" /></ac:image></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005293 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00180: (2026x Refresh1) Reviewing validation results

- page_id: `304005293`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005293/2026x+Refresh1+Reviewing+validation+results
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:26.351+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Validation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can review validation results via:

- for singular or multiple elements.
- for singular elements.
- for singular elements.
- for multiple elements (only in views).

##### Severity level

The table below explains the severity levels of the failed validation rules. This information is displayed in views, the ****, and the ****.

| Icon | Severity level | Severity level priority | Highlight color |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Fatal | 1 (i.e., the highest) | red |
| Error | 2 |  |  |
|  | Warning | 3 | yellow |
|  | Info | 4 | blue |
| Debug | 5 (i.e., the lowest) |  |  |

However, the icons and highlight colors are different in the Textual Editor; see the table below.

| Icon in Textual Editor | Explanation | Highlight color |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Syntax error | red |
|  | Severity level: Fatal, Error | yellow |
|  | Severity level: Warning, Info, Debug | blue |

The severity level priority prioritizes more severe failures over lesser ones. This is reflected in the **Validation Results pop-up in views** and the **** marker eye, which display the icon or color of the failed validation rule with the highest severity level priority.

##### Validation Results panel

The Validation Results panel displays validation failures for:

- Singular element in a view, if opened via the Validation Results pop-up.
- Multiple elements in a view, if opened via the Open Validation Results for the View [ATTACHMENT filename='Open Validation Results for the View.png'] button.
- Specific elements and their recursive contents in the model, if opened via the Validate command.

###### Validation Results panel areas

The Validation Results panel consists of the following areas:

- Validation Results panel title bar displays either the name of the singular element or the view for which the panel is opened.
- Validation results area displays the validation results columns:
  - Element . The element failing a validation rule. This is the element whose symbol is highlighted in a view. The elements in the column are grouped based on the failed validation rule's suite. Right-click an element in the **Element**column to access its shortcut menu commands:**Specification** to open the element in the Specification panel.**Select in Containment tree**to select the element in the Containment tree.**Open in Textual Editor**to open the element in the Textual Editor.
  - Severity. The failed validation rule's **severity** level .
  - Message . The failed validation rule's message explaining the failure.
    - (Conditional) The message may list element(s) causing the validation failure of the highlighted element. The element is indicated as a hyperlink; click the link to select the element in the Containment tree. This information is displayed only if it is relevant to the specific validation rule.
      - If the validation fails the highlighted element's owned element, the hyperlink is indicated before the validation message.
  - Name . The name of the failed validation rule. In the Validation Results pop-up, this name is displayed in parentheses.
- Panel footer displays a summary of validation results, indicating the number of failed validation rules for each severity level.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Validation Results panel

If you modify the view or the element(s) for which the **Validation Results panel**was opened, you need to reopen the panel to update its results.

To open the Validation Results panel

- From a view:
  - For a singular element in a view via the Open Validation Results for the View button:
    1. In the view's toolbar, click the Open Validation Results for the View [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The Validation Results panel opens, displaying validation results for all failing elements in the view.
  - For multiple elements in a view via the Validation Results pop-up:
    1. In a view, click the highlighted element symbol. In its smart manipulator, click the Show Validation Results button. The Validation Results pop-up appears.
    2. Click the Open in Validation Results button at the bottom of the pop-up to open the results in the Validation Results panel. The Validation Results panel opens, displaying validation results for the selected failing element.
- From a view or the Containment tree:
  - For specific elements and their recursive contents in the model via the Validate command:
    1. Right-click an element in the Containment tree or an element symbol in a view and in the shortcut menu, click Validate . The Validation Results panel opens, displaying validation results for any failing elements in the selected scope.

##### Validation Results pop-up in Textual Editor

- To validate the textual notation in the Textual Editor, ensure the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Environment options' space='']**Run Validation** environment option is enabled (default). The namespace for which you open the Textual Editor is validated automatically.
- The validation failure icons displayed in the left margin of the Textual Editor, as well as the color of the element underline and the scroll bar markers, correspond to the of the failed validation rule.

You can locate the failing elements in the Textual Editor in two ways:

- The failing element's notation is underlined.
- The Textual Editor's scroll bar is supplemented with markers indicating the failing element's location in the editor.

Hovering over the failing element displays a pop-up with the validation results that contain:

- Message . The failed validation rule's message explaining the failure.
  - The message is followed by the name of the failed validation rule, which is greyed out.
  - (Conditional) The message may list element(s) causing the validation failure of the highlighted element. The element is indicated as a hyperlink; click the link to select the element in the Textual Editor. This information is displayed only if it is relevant to the specific validation rule. **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**
- You can click the View Problem button in the Validation Results pop-up or press Alt+F8 to expand the pop-up, allowing you to navigate between all elements that fail the validation rule as a result (e.g., due to inheritance).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Validation Results pop-up in views

Failing elements are highlighted in views. The failing element's smart manipulator has the **Show Validation Results**button, which displays a pop-up with the element's validation results. The button's icon depends on the of the failed validation rules. If the element fails multiple rules with different severity levels, the icon and the element highlighting in the view indicate the highest severity level of the failed validation rules.

The Validation Results pop-up displays:

- The failed validation rule's **severity** level icon .
- The failed validation rule's message explaining the failure.
  - (Conditional) The message may list element(s) causing the validation failure of the highlighted element. The element is indicated as a hyperlink; click the link to select the element in the Containment tree. This information is displayed only if it is relevant to the specific validation rule.
    - If the validation fails the highlighted element's owned element, the hyperlink is indicated before the validation message.
- The failed validation rule's name . The name is indicated in parentheses.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Reviewing validation results via the Validation Results pop-up

To review validation results via the Validation Results pop-up

1. In a view, click the highlighted element symbol. In its smart manipulator, click the Show Validation Results button. The Validation Results pop-up appears.
2. (Optional) In the Validation Results pop-up :
  1. In the Validation Results pop-up, click the hyperlinked element causing the validation failure to select it in the Containment tree.
  2. Click the Open in Validation Results button at the bottom of the pop-up to open the results in the Validation Results panel.

##### Validation Results marker bar

The validation results marker bar appears on the right side of the view pane if the view contains symbols of failing elements. The marker bar shrinks to fit the screen and displays the relative positions of the element symbols not visible on the screen; scroll through the pane to see them.

- Every marker is colored according to the .
- Hovering over the marker displays the validation message; clicking it selects the appropriate symbol on the view pane.
- Hovering over the marker eye at the top of the marker bar displays the summary of the view's validation results.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

This information is displayed only if it is relevant to the specific validation rule. <ul><li>If the validation fails the highlighted element's owned element, the hyperlink is indicated before the validation message.</li></ul></li></ul></li><li><strong>Name</strong>. The name of the failed validation rule. In the Validation Results pop-up, this name is displayed in parentheses.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="b26348ae-c7b5-477d-b262-cf81201b8b17"><strong>Panel footer</strong> displays a summary of validation results, indicating the number of failed validation rules for each severity level.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Validation Results panel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Reviewing validation results" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4>Opening the Validation Results panel</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="77e5be0e-0311-4553-b64e-ef8cbfa5db1d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you modify the view or the element(s) for which the <strong>Validation Results panel </strong>was opened, you need to reopen the panel to update its results.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To open the Validation Results panel</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="3b4fbc54-b642-466d-8536-e396815d042a">From a view:<ul><li data-uuid="34d4358b-b2b7-4618-8a19-d167e171932b">For a singular element in a view via the <strong>Open Validation Results for the View </strong>button:<ol><li>In the view's toolbar, click the <strong>Open Validation Results for the View <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Open Validation Results for the View.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Reviewing validation results" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>button. <br />The <strong>Validation Results </strong>panel opens, displaying validation results for all failing elements in the view.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="0e5c5b76-ae10-4cc7-96f3-cf191817351a">For multiple elements in a view via the <strong>Validation Results </strong>pop-up:<ol><li>In a view, click the highlighted element symbol. In its smart manipulator, click the <strong>Show Validation Results<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span></strong>button. The <strong>Validation Results</strong> pop-up appears.</li><li>Click the <strong>Open in Validation Results </strong>button at the bottom of the pop-up to open the results in the <strong>Validation Results </strong>panel.<br />The <strong>Validation Results </strong>panel opens, displaying validation results for the selected failing element.</li></ol></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="09317912-8c87-4320-b881-e60e33ee8c63">From a view or the Containment tree:<ul><li data-uuid="5a2166e2-0149-40f3-a39f-1ed86dd875d3">For specific elements and their recursive contents in the model via the <strong>Validate </strong>command:<ol><li>Right-click an element in the Containment tree or an element symbol in a view and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Validate</strong>.<br />The <strong>Validation Results </strong>panel opens, displaying validation results for any failing elements in the selected scope.</li></ol></li></ul></li></ul><h3>Validation Results pop-up in Textual Editor</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="810ea20f-9fa8-468d-8088-e81bffbdfa91"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="a3dad45b-860a-47c3-91f0-4cfb5da0938e">To validate the textual notation in the Textual Editor, ensure the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Environment options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Run Validation</strong> environment option</ac:link-body></ac:link> is enabled (default). The namespace for which you open the Textual Editor is validated automatically.</li><li data-uuid="4e5bc416-5b74-478b-a66c-84285d8b2d3c">The validation failure icons displayed in the left margin of the Textual Editor, as well as the color of the element underline and the scroll bar markers, correspond to the <ac:link ac:anchor="Severity level"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[severity level]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the failed validation rule.   </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can locate the failing elements in the Textual Editor in two ways:</p><ul><li data-uuid="127efcf9-2f14-43c0-8578-b5e659b9cd7d">The failing element's notation is underlined.</li><li data-uuid="c6818eee-f003-4465-b578-487e94e085b0">The Textual Editor's scroll bar is supplemented with markers indicating the failing element's location in the editor. </li></ul><p> Hovering over the failing element displays a pop-up with the validation results that contain:</p><ul><li data-uuid="74e701ce-d673-48d4-895d-3d0c2e51aa09"><strong>Message</strong>. The failed validation rule's message explaining the failure.<ul><li data-uuid="9fbbb2a4-9834-4c8d-958c-d13ce7d1ebd4">The message is followed by the <strong>name</strong> of the failed validation rule, which is greyed out.</li><li data-uuid="17c62eef-e13b-4e60-a1bf-329862a93d4d">(Conditional) The message may list element(s) causing the validation failure of the highlighted element. The element is indicated as a hyperlink; click the link to select the element in the Textual Editor. This information is displayed only if it is relevant to the specific validation rule. <br /><em><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="element(s) causing the validation failure of the highlighted element.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Reviewing validation results" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></em></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="965e18f0-9a18-4e4d-8354-5303c3f32d59">You can click the <strong>View Problem </strong>button in the <strong>Validation Results pop-up </strong>or press Alt+F8 to expand the pop-up, allowing you to navigate between all elements that fail the validation rule as a result (e.g., due to inheritance).</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Validation Results pop-up in Textual Editor.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Reviewing validation results" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Validation Results pop-up in views</h3><p>Failing elements are highlighted in views. The failing element's smart manipulator has the <strong>Show Validation Results </strong>button, which displays a pop-up with the element's validation results. The button's icon depends on the <ac:link ac:anchor="Severity level"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[severity level]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the failed validation rules. If the element fails multiple rules with different severity levels, the icon and the element highlighting in the view indicate the highest severity level of the failed validation rules.</p><p>The Validation Results pop-up displays:</p><ul><li data-uuid="b86d6f2e-8f49-45e3-9409-bfc39f1d3da9">The failed validation rule's <ac:link ac:anchor="Severity level"><ac:link-body><strong>severity</strong> level</ac:link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> icon</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span></li><li data-uuid="35fe2e85-72f7-42ae-8861-731eea1ba537">The failed validation rule's <strong>message</strong> explaining the failure.<ul><li data-uuid="9341e41d-643f-44b0-9169-0c281af89c7e">(Conditional) The message may list element(s) causing the validation failure of the highlighted element. The element is indicated as a hyperlink; click the link to select the element in the Containment tree. This information is displayed only if it is relevant to the specific validation rule. <ul><li data-uuid="ca89ca94-27be-479b-897f-69ce32777a3e">If the validation fails the highlighted element's owned element, the hyperlink is indicated before the validation message.</li></ul></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="e007187e-6a55-4bad-b722-9c32a0be834c">The failed validation rule's <strong>name</strong>. The name is indicated in parentheses.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Validation Results pop-up.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Reviewing validation results" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4 style="text-align: left;">Reviewing validation results via the Validation Results pop-up</h4><p style="text-align: left;">To review validation results via the Validation Results pop-up</p><hr /><ol><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="f89b36f9-82f2-4894-9dee-0ef7f6709802">In a view, click the highlighted element symbol. In its smart manipulator, click the <strong>Show Validation Results<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span></strong>button. The <strong>Validation Results</strong> pop-up appears.</li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="d3d4f313-ef2a-42a0-a19d-c1738e191e50">(Optional) In the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Validation Results</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> pop-up</span>:<ol><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="7dfe6938-8a18-4ef4-83d2-60c8f7bdd9b9">In the <strong>Validation Results</strong> pop-up, click the hyperlinked element causing the validation failure to select it in the Containment tree.</li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="49a0e796-31ed-457e-9029-00e137615107">Click the <strong>Open in Validation Results </strong>button at the bottom of the pop-up to open the results in the <strong>Validation Results </strong>panel.</li></ol></li></ol><h3>Validation Results marker bar</h3><p>The validation results marker bar appears on the right side of the view pane if the view contains symbols of failing elements. The marker bar shrinks to fit the screen and displays the relative positions of the element symbols not visible on the screen; scroll through the pane to see them.</p><ul><li data-uuid="47e9fb77-f7d3-4d40-9db3-ad693cccb9b1">Every marker is colored according to the <ac:link ac:anchor="Severity level"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[severity level]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="90e0fec3-76f5-478d-a4ec-1b528e534cb2">Hovering over the marker displays the validation message; clicking it selects the appropriate symbol on the view pane.</li><li data-uuid="49c200a5-84d8-4c06-a072-be4315e8920d">Hovering over the marker eye at the top of the marker bar displays the summary of the view's validation results.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Validation results marker bar.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Reviewing validation results" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004699 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00181: (2026x Refresh1) Satisfy requirement

- page_id: `304004699`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004699/2026x+Refresh1+Satisfy+requirement
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:17:42.738+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Requirement satisfaction is modeled via the*satisfy requirement usage*element, which asserts that a requirement is satisfied when a given feature is bound to the subject parameter of the requirement. Other parameters or features of the requirement may also be bound in the body of the satisfy requirement usage.A satisfy requirement usage can also be negated. A negated satisfy requirement usage asserts that some entity does not satisfy the given requirement.

There are two ways a requirement can be satisfied by another element:

50%

- by referencing the requirement usage defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the satisfy keyword (unnamed element) or the satisfy requirement keyword (named element) . [ATTACHMENT filename='Satisfy_requirement_2.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='Satisfy_requirement_1.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 

50%

- by containing an owned satisfy requirement usage, i.e., the satisfied requirement is the satisfy requirement usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the satisfy requirement keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='Satisfy_requirement_3.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

##### Satisfying a requirement by referencing a requirement usage defined elsewhere in the model

###### Satisfying requirements via the Textual Editor

To satisfy a requirement via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the part element and do one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed satisfy requirement usage, declare the *satisfy* keyword followed by the name of the requirement the part satisfies.
  2. For a named satisfy requirement usage, declare the *satisfy requirement* keyword, specify the element name, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Reference subsetting' space=''] the requirement the part satisfies. To negate a satisfy requirement usage, specify the *not*keyword before the satisfy keyword, e.g., *not satisfy*.
2. (Optional) To specify a requirement with an explicit satisfying feature, state the by keyword and the name of the subject element that satisfies the requirement.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. requirementvehicleSpecification{// the referenced requirementsubjectvehicle:Vehicle;actorenvironment:Environment;requirementfullVehicleMassLimit:MassRequirement{subjectvehicle:Vehicle;attribute:>>massRequired=2000[kilogram];}}// step 1.a - unnamed satisfy requirement usage with an implicit satisfying featurepartvehicle_c1:Vehicle{ 
satisfyvehicleSpecification.fullVehicleMassLimit;// the satisfying feature is implicitly the part *vehicle_c1* 
}// step 1.b - named satisfy requirement usage with an implicit satisfying featurepartvehicle_c1:Vehicle{ 
satisfyrequirementsatisfiedReq::>vehicleSpecification.fullVehicleMassLimit;// the satisfying feature is implicitly the part *vehicle_c1*}// step 2 - unnamed satisfy requirement usage with an explicit satisfying featurepartvehicle:Vehicle{ 
partvehicle_c2:Vehicle; 
satisfyvehicleSpecification.fullVehicleMassLimitbyvehicle_c2; 
}// negated satisfy requirement usage 
partvehicle_c1:Vehicle{ 
notsatisfyvehicleSpecification.fullVehicleMassLimit; 
}

###### Satisfying requirements via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To satisfy a requirement via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop a requirement element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on:
  - a part element symbol that you want to satisfy the requirement by referencing it using the Create Satisfy Requirement command.
  - a satisfy requirement usage symbol that you want to satisfy by referencing it using the **Set Satisfied Requirement****** command.

###### Satisfying requirements via the Specification panel

To satisfy a requirement via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop a requirement element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on the **Satisfied Requirement** field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the satisfy requirement usage. The appropriate requirement is specified for the element.

###### Satisfying requirements via the view palette

To satisfy requirements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Requirements group, click satisfy . If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the**satisfy requirement**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. To satisfy an existing requirement, in the view pane, click on the part element symbol, then on the requirement element symbol.
  2. To satisfy a new requirement, in the view pane, click on the part element symbol, then elsewhere in the view pane to create a requirement element symbol. The satisfy path is drawn between the element symbols.

###### Satisfying requirements via the smart manipulator in a view

To satisfy requirements via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view , do one of the following:
  - To satisfy an existing requirement, select the part element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the satisfy command. Click on the requirement element symbol.
  - To create a new satisfy requirement, select the part element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the satisfy command. Click elsewhere in the view pane to create a requirement element symbol . The satisfy path is drawn between the element symbols.

###### Satisfying requirementsvia the Create Relation command

To satisfy requirements via the Create Relation command

1. Right-click on the client (source) element.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click satisfy . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the supplier (target). Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the supplier (target) and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the supplier (target) from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

##### Satisfying a requirementvia the ownedsatisfy requirementusageitselfas thesatisfied requirement

###### Satisfying requirements via the Textual Editor2Satisfying requirements via the Textual Editor

To satisfy a requirement via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the part element and do one of the following:
  1. To specify a requirement without the satisfying feature, declare the satisfy requirement keyword followed by the qualified name of the requirement the part satisfies.
  2. To specify a requirement with the satisfying feature declare the satisfy requirement keyword followed by the qualified name of the satisfied requirement, then state the by keyword and the name of the subject element that satisfies the requirement. To negate a satisfy requirement usage, specify the *not* keyword before the satisfy keyword, e.g., *not satisfy*.
2. Click the **Synchronize**button. partvehicle_c1:Vehicle{ 
satisfy requirementvehicleSpecification;// satisfied requirement is the defined satisfy requirement usage itself contained by the parent part that is the implicit satisfying feature 
}

###### Satisfying requirements via the Create Element command

To satisfy a requirement via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select satisfy requirement .
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the satisfy requirement.

###### Satisfying requirements via the view palette2Satisfying requirements via the view palette

To satisfy a requirement via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Requirements/Constraints group, click satisfy requirement .
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the element symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the satisfy requirement.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

}<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// step 1.a - unnamed satisfy requirement
